## Sparse (info rsq 90): Baseline vs Masked

```{r}

library(data.table)
library(scales)
library(ggplot2)
library(cowplot)
library(stringr)
library(foreach)
library(doMC)
registerDoMC(8)

# --- 1. Data Processing Loop ---
# This section is an enhanced version of your original code.

# Define analysis parameters
populations <- c('EUR')
traits <- c('HT', 'HB', 'PLT', 'BMI', 'BWT', 'MCHC', 'SBP', 'NEU', 'HDL', 'TC')
base_dir_baseline <- '~/pgs_wgs/sparse/baseline/'
base_dir_masked <- '~/pgs_wgs/sparse/masked/'
pheno_dir <- '/mnt/project/mind_the_gap/phenotype/'
props <- c(0.05, 0.25, 0.5)

n_boot <- 5 # Number of bootstrap replicates
set.seed(123)  # For reproducible results

# Create the list to hold all results
final_results_list <- list()

# --- 2. RUN SEQUENTIAL OUTER LOOP (for pop) ---
for (pop in populations) {
    print(paste("Processing Population:", pop))
  for (prop in props) {
    print(paste("Processing prop:", prop))
    
    baseline_file <- paste0(base_dir_baseline, 'ukb.', pop, '.sparse_rsq_90.sscore')
    masked_file <- paste0(base_dir_masked, 'ukb.', pop, '.sparse_rsq_90.prop_', prop, '.sscore')
    
    if (!file.exists(baseline_file) || !file.exists(masked_file)) {
        print(paste("Skipping pop:", pop, "- files not found"))
        next 
    }
    
    pgs_baseline <- fread(baseline_file)
    pgs_masked <- fread(masked_file)

    pgs_baseline <- pgs_baseline[pgs_baseline$IID %in% pgs_masked$IID, ]
    pgs_masked <- pgs_masked[pgs_masked$IID %in% pgs_baseline$IID, ]

    # --- 3. RUN PARALLEL INNER LOOP (for trait) ---
    trait_results_list <- foreach(
        trait = traits,
        .combine = 'append', 
        .packages = 'data.table',
        .export = c('pheno_dir', 'n_boot', 'pgs_baseline', 'pgs_masked', 'pop') 
    ) %dopar% {
        
        pheno_file <- paste0(pheno_dir, trait, '.', pop, '.txt')
        if (!file.exists(pheno_file)) {
            return(list()) 
        }
        
        pheno <- fread(pheno_file)

        # Subset PGS data
        pgs_baseline_i <- pgs_baseline[, c('IID', names(pgs_baseline)[grepl('SUM', names(pgs_baseline)) &
                                                                        grepl(trait, names(pgs_baseline))]), with = F]
        pgs_masked_i <- pgs_masked[, c('IID', names(pgs_masked)[grepl('SUM', names(pgs_masked)) &
                                                                    grepl(trait, names(pgs_masked))]), with = F]
        
        # Merge PGS data
        pgs_baseline_pheno <- merge(pheno, pgs_baseline_i, by = 'IID', sort = FALSE)
        pgs_masked_pheno <- merge(pheno, pgs_masked_i, by = 'IID', sort = FALSE)
        
        # --- NEW: CRITICAL ALIGNMENT STEP ---
        # Ensure both data.tables have the same individuals in the same order
        common_iids <- intersect(pgs_baseline_pheno$IID, pgs_masked_pheno$IID)
        pgs_baseline_pheno <- pgs_baseline_pheno[IID %in% common_iids][order(IID)]
        pgs_masked_pheno <- pgs_masked_pheno[IID %in% common_iids][order(IID)]
        # Now, row 'i' in one table matches row 'i' in the other
        
        param_cols <- names(pgs_baseline_i)[-1]
        
        param_results_list <- list() 

        for (param in param_cols) {
            if (!(param %in% names(pgs_masked_pheno))) next
            
            n <- nrow(pgs_baseline_pheno)
            if (n == 0) next 
            
            # --- 1.5. BOOTSTRAPPING SECTION ---
            boot_stats <- replicate(n_boot, {
                indices <- sample(1:n, n, replace = TRUE)
                
                # Thanks to alignment, we can use indices on both
                boot_base <- pgs_baseline_pheno[indices]
                boot_masked <- pgs_masked_pheno[indices]
                
                # And we can use a single pheno vector
                pheno_vals_boot <- boot_base[['outcome']]
                pgs_vals_base <- boot_base[[param]]
                pgs_vals_masked <- boot_masked[[param]]
                
                # R2 stats
                r2_baseline_boot <- tryCatch(cor(pheno_vals_boot, pgs_vals_base)^2, error = function(e) NA)
                r2_masked_boot <- tryCatch(cor(pheno_vals_boot, pgs_vals_masked)^2, error = function(e) NA)
                
                # Relative Variance
                var_baseline_boot <- tryCatch(var(pgs_vals_base), error = function(e) NA)
                var_masked_boot <- tryCatch(var(pgs_vals_masked), error = function(e) NA)
                rel_var_boot <- if (is.na(var_baseline_boot) || is.na(var_masked_boot) || var_baseline_boot == 0) {
                    NA
                } else {
                    var_masked_boot / var_baseline_boot
                }
                
                # Correlation stat
                cor_baseline_masked_boot <- tryCatch(cor(pgs_vals_base, pgs_vals_masked), error = function(e) NA)
                cor_baseline_masked_sq_boot <- cor_baseline_masked_boot^2
                
                # Relative R2
                rel_r2_boot <- if (is.na(r2_baseline_boot) || is.na(r2_masked_boot) || r2_baseline_boot == 0) {
                    NA
                } else {
                    r2_masked_boot / r2_baseline_boot
                }
                
                # Return a named vector
                c(
                    r2_baseline = r2_baseline_boot,
                    r2_masked = r2_masked_boot,
                    rel_var = rel_var_boot,
                    cor_baseline_masked = cor_baseline_masked_boot,
                    cor_baseline_masked_sq = cor_baseline_masked_sq_boot,
                    rel_r2 = rel_r2_boot
                )
            })
            
            # Transpose boot_stats
            boot_stats_t <- t(boot_stats)
            
            # --- Calculate 95% CIs ---
            safe_quantile <- function(col_name) {
                # Add an extra safety check for all-NA columns
                if (!col_name %in% colnames(boot_stats_t) || all(is.na(boot_stats_t[, col_name]))) {
                    return(c(NA, NA))
                }
                quantile(boot_stats_t[, col_name], c(0.025, 0.975), na.rm = TRUE)
            }
            
            r2_baseline_ci <- safe_quantile("r2_baseline")
            r2_masked_ci <- safe_quantile("r2_masked")
            rel_var_ci <- safe_quantile("rel_var")
            cor_baseline_masked_ci <- safe_quantile("cor_baseline_masked")
            cor_baseline_masked_sq_ci <- safe_quantile("cor_baseline_masked_sq")
            rel_r2_ci <- safe_quantile("rel_r2")
            
            # --- 1.6. Calculate Point Estimates ---
            pheno_point <- pgs_baseline_pheno[['outcome']]
            pgs_s_point <- pgs_baseline_pheno[[param]]
            pgs_s_prime_point <- pgs_masked_pheno[[param]]
            
            r2_baseline_pheno <- cor(pheno_point, pgs_s_point)^2
            r2_masked_pheno <- cor(pheno_point, pgs_s_prime_point)^2
            p_val <- coef(summary(lm(pheno_point ~ pgs_s_point)))[2,4]
            cor_baseline_masked <- cor(pgs_s_point, pgs_s_prime_point)
            cor_baseline_masked_sq <- cor_baseline_masked^2
            
            var_baseline <- var(pgs_s_point)
            var_masked <- var(pgs_s_prime_point)
            rel_var_point <- var_masked / var_baseline

            # --- 1.7. Store results ---
            param_results_list[[length(param_results_list) + 1]] <- data.table(
                param = param,
                trait = trait,
                population = pop,
                prop = prop,
                r2_baseline_pheno = r2_baseline_pheno,
                r2_masked_pheno = r2_masked_pheno,
                cor_baseline_masked = cor_baseline_masked,
                cor_baseline_masked_sq = cor_baseline_masked_sq,
                obs_rel_r2 = r2_masked_pheno / r2_baseline_pheno,
                obs_rel_var = rel_var_point,
                p_baseline_pheno = p_val,
                n = nrow(pgs_baseline_pheno),
                r2_baseline_low_ci = r2_baseline_ci[1],
                r2_baseline_high_ci = r2_baseline_ci[2],
                r2_masked_low_ci = r2_masked_ci[1],
                r2_masked_high_ci = r2_masked_ci[2],
                rel_var_low_ci = rel_var_ci[1],
                rel_var_high_ci = rel_var_ci[2],
                cor_baseline_masked_low_ci = cor_baseline_masked_ci[1],
                cor_baseline_masked_high_ci = cor_baseline_masked_ci[2],
                cor_baseline_masked_sq_low_ci = cor_baseline_masked_sq_ci[1],
                cor_baseline_masked_sq_high_ci = cor_baseline_masked_sq_ci[2],
                rel_r2_low_ci = rel_r2_ci[1],
                rel_r2_high_ci = rel_r2_ci[2]
            )
        } # End of param loop
        
        # The parallel worker returns the list of data.tables for this trait
        return(param_results_list) 
        
    } # End of parallel trait loop

    # --- 4. COMBINE RESULTS (within sequential loop) ---
    if (length(trait_results_list) > 0) {
        final_results_list <- append(final_results_list, trait_results_list)
    }
  } # End of prop loop

} # End of sequential pop loop

results_dt <- rbindlist(final_results_list)
results_dt[, pgs_method := ifelse(str_detect(param, 'sbayesrc'), 'SBayesRC', 'pT+clump')]
results_dt$call_rate<-paste0('Call rate = ', 1-results_dt$prop)

write.csv(results_dt, '~/wgs_mvp_sparse_rsq_90_results.csv', row.names=F)
system('dx upload ~/wgs_mvp_sparse_rsq_90_results.csv --path mind_the_gap/results/')
results_dt <- fread('/mnt/project/mind_the_gap/results/wgs_mvp_sparse_rsq_90_results.csv')

# Plot the relative R2 between baseline and masked PGS
ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC' & results_dt$population == 'EUR', ], 
       aes(x = trait, y = obs_rel_r2, colour = trait)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_r2_low_ci, 
                    ymax = rel_r2_high_ci)) +
  facet_grid(. ~ call_rate) +
#  coord_fixed() +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = "Trait", 
       y = "Observed relative R2 (95%CI)",
       colour = NULL)

# Plot the relative variance between baseline and masked PGS
ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC', ], 
       aes(x = trait, y = obs_rel_var, colour = trait)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_var_low_ci, 
                    ymax = rel_var_high_ci)) +
  facet_grid(. ~ call_rate) +
#  coord_fixed() +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = "Trait", 
       y = "Observed relative variance (95%CI)",
       colour = NULL)
# Masked PGS have substantially lower variance as expected

# Compare squared correlation between PGS and observed relative R2
p2 <- ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC' & results_dt$population == 'EUR', ], 
       aes(x = cor_baseline_masked_sq, y = obs_rel_r2, colour = trait)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_abline(colour = 'red') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = cor_baseline_masked_sq_low_ci, 
                    xmax = cor_baseline_masked_sq_high_ci), width = 0) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_r2_low_ci, 
                    ymax = rel_r2_high_ci), width = 0) +
  facet_grid(. ~ call_rate) +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = expression(Observed~Cor(s, s*minute)^2),
        y = expression(Observed~R[s*minute]^2 / R[s]^2),
        colour = NULL)

# Compare squared correlation between PGS and observed relative R2
p1 <- ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'pT+clump' & population == 'EUR' & grepl('0_1_', results_dt$param), ], 
       aes(x = cor_baseline_masked_sq, y = obs_rel_r2, colour = trait)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_abline(colour = 'red') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = cor_baseline_masked_sq_low_ci, 
                    xmax = cor_baseline_masked_sq_high_ci), width = 0) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_r2_low_ci, 
                    ymax = rel_r2_high_ci), width = 0) +
  facet_grid(. ~ call_rate) +
#  coord_fixed() +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = expression(Observed~Cor(s, s*minute)^2),
        y = expression(Observed~R[s*minute]^2 / R[s]^2),
       colour = NULL)

# Add titles to p1 and p2
p1 <- p1 + labs(title = 'pT+clump: INFO-based masking')
p2 <- p2 + labs(title = 'SBayesRC: INFO-based masking')

png('~/obs_rel_r2_comp_info_masking_ptclump_mvp_rsq_90.png', res = 300, width = 2750, height = 2500, units = 'px')
plot_grid(p1, p2, labels = c('A', 'B'), ncol = 1)
dev.off()
system('dx upload ~/obs_rel_r2_comp_info_masking_ptclump_mvp_rsq_90.png --path mind_the_gap/results/')

```

***
## Dense: Baseline vs Masked

```{r}

library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(cowplot)
library(stringr)
library(foreach)
library(doMC)
registerDoMC(8)

# --- 1. Data Processing Loop ---
# This section is an enhanced version of your original code.

# Define analysis parameters
populations <- c('AFR', 'EUR')
traits <- c('HT', 'HB', 'PLT', 'BMI', 'BWT', 'MCHC', 'SBP', 'NEU', 'HDL', 'TC')
base_dir_baseline <- '~/pgs_wgs/dense/baseline/'
base_dir_masked <- '~/pgs_wgs/dense/masked/'
pheno_dir <- '/mnt/project/mind_the_gap/phenotype/'

n_boot <- 1000 # Number of bootstrap replicates (1000 is good, 100 for testing)
set.seed(123)  # For reproducible results

# Create the list to hold all results
final_results_list <- list()

# --- 2. RUN SEQUENTIAL OUTER LOOP (for pop) ---
# We use a standard 'for' loop for the sequential part
for (pop in populations) {
    
    # --- This code runs sequentially on the main thread, once per pop ---
    print(paste("Processing Population:", pop))
    baseline_file <- paste0(base_dir_baseline, 'ukb.', pop, '.dense.sscore')
    masked_file <- paste0(base_dir_masked, 'ukb.', pop, '.dense.sscore')
    
    if (!file.exists(baseline_file) || !file.exists(masked_file)) {
        print(paste("Skipping pop:", pop, "- files not found"))
        next # Use 'next' to skip to the next 'pop' in a 'for' loop
    }
    
    pgs_baseline <- fread(baseline_file)
    pgs_masked <- fread(masked_file)

    pgs_baseline <- pgs_baseline[pgs_baseline$IID %in% pgs_masked$IID, ]
    pgs_masked <- pgs_masked[pgs_masked$IID %in% pgs_baseline$IID, ]
    # --- End of sequential loading part ---


    # --- 3. RUN PARALLEL INNER LOOP (for trait) ---
    # Now, call foreach %dopar% to process all traits for THIS pop in parallel.
    # The '.export' is crucial to send the loaded data to the workers.
    trait_results_list <- foreach(
        trait = traits,
        .combine = 'append', # Appends the lists returned by each 'trait'
        .packages = 'data.table',
        .export = c('pheno_dir', 'n_boot', 'est', 'pgs_baseline', 'pgs_masked', 'pop') 
    ) %dopar% {
        
        pheno_file <- paste0(pheno_dir, trait, '.', pop, '.txt')
        if (!file.exists(pheno_file)) {
            return(list()) # Skip this trait, return an empty list
        }
        
        pheno <- fread(pheno_file)

        # Subset PGS data
        pgs_baseline_i <- pgs_baseline[, c('IID', names(pgs_baseline)[grepl('SUM', names(pgs_baseline)) &
                                                                        grepl(trait, names(pgs_baseline))]), with = F]
        pgs_masked_i <- pgs_masked[, c('IID', names(pgs_masked)[grepl('SUM', names(pgs_masked)) &
                                                                    grepl(trait, names(pgs_masked))]), with = F]
        
        # Merge PGS data
        pgs_baseline_pheno <- merge(pheno, pgs_baseline_i, by = 'IID', sort = FALSE)
        pgs_masked_pheno <- merge(pheno, pgs_masked_i, by = 'IID', sort = FALSE)
        
        param_cols <- names(pgs_baseline_i)[-1]
        
        # This list will hold results for all 'param's for THIS trait
        param_results_list <- list() 

        for (param in param_cols) {
            if (!(param %in% names(pgs_masked_pheno))) next
            
            n <- nrow(pgs_baseline_pheno)
            if (n == 0) next # Safety check
            
            # --- 1.5. BOOTSTRAPPING SECTION ---
            boot_stats <- replicate(n_boot, {
                indices <- sample(1:n, n, replace = TRUE)
                
                boot_base <- pgs_baseline_pheno[indices]
                boot_masked <- pgs_masked_pheno[indices]
                
                pheno_vals_base <- boot_base[[trait]]
                pgs_vals_base <- boot_base[[param]]
                
                pheno_vals_masked <- boot_masked[[trait]]
                pgs_vals_masked <- boot_masked[[param]]
                
                # R2 stats
                r2_base_boot <- tryCatch(cor(pheno_vals_base, pgs_vals_base)^2, error = function(e) NA)
                r2_masked_boot <- tryCatch(cor(pheno_vals_masked, pgs_vals_masked)^2, error = function(e) NA)
                
                # Relative Variance
                var_base_boot <- tryCatch(var(pgs_vals_base), error = function(e) NA)
                var_masked_boot <- tryCatch(var(pgs_vals_masked), error = function(e) NA)
                rel_var_boot <- if (is.na(var_base_boot) || is.na(var_masked_boot) || var_base_boot == 0) {
                    NA
                } else {
                    var_masked_boot / var_base_boot
                }
                
                # Correlation stat
                cor_base_masked_boot <- tryCatch(cor(pgs_vals_base, pgs_vals_masked), error = function(e) NA)
                cor_base_masked_sq_boot <- cor_base_masked_boot^2
                
                # Relative R2
                rel_r2_boot <- if (is.na(r2_base_boot) || is.na(r2_masked_boot) || r2_base_boot == 0) {
                    NA
                } else {
                    r2_masked_boot / r2_base_boot
                }
                
                # Return a named vector
                c(
                    r2_base = r2_base_boot,
                    r2_masked = r2_masked_boot,
                    rel_var = rel_var_boot,
                    cor_base_masked = cor_base_masked_boot,
                    cor_base_masked_sq = cor_base_masked_sq_boot,
                    rel_r2 = rel_r2_boot
                )
            })
            
            # Transpose boot_stats
            boot_stats_t <- t(boot_stats)
            
            # --- Calculate 95% CIs ---
            safe_quantile <- function(col_name) {
                quantile(boot_stats_t[, col_name], c(0.025, 0.975), na.rm = TRUE)
            }
            
            r2_base_ci <- safe_quantile("r2_base")
            r2_masked_ci <- safe_quantile("r2_masked")
            rel_var_ci <- safe_quantile("rel_var")
            cor_base_masked_ci <- safe_quantile("cor_base_masked")
            cor_base_masked_sq_ci <- safe_quantile("cor_base_masked_sq")
            rel_r2_ci <- safe_quantile("rel_r2")
            # --- END BOOTSTRAPPING ---
            
            # --- 1.6. Calculate Point Estimates ---
            r2_baseline_pheno <- cor(pgs_baseline_pheno[[trait]], pgs_baseline_pheno[[param]])^2
            r2_masked_pheno <- cor(pgs_masked_pheno[[trait]], pgs_masked_pheno[[param]])^2
            p_val <- coef(summary(lm(pgs_baseline_pheno[[trait]] ~ pgs_baseline_pheno[[param]])))[2,4]
            cor_base_masked <- cor(pgs_baseline_pheno[[param]], pgs_masked_pheno[[param]])
            cor_baseline_masked_sq <- cor_base_masked^2
            
            var_baseline <- var(pgs_baseline_pheno[[param]])
            var_masked <- var(pgs_masked_pheno[[param]])
            rel_var_point <- var_masked / var_baseline

            est_i <- est[est$beta_set == gsub('_SUM','',param),]
            
            # --- 1.7. Store results ---
            param_results_list[[length(param_results_list) + 1]] <- data.table(
                param = param,
                trait = trait,
                population = pop,
                r2_baseline_pheno = r2_baseline_pheno,
                r2_masked_pheno = r2_masked_pheno,
                cor_baseline_masked = cor_base_masked,
                cor_baseline_masked_sq = cor_baseline_masked_sq,
                obs_rel_r2 = r2_masked_pheno / r2_baseline_pheno,
                obs_rel_var = rel_var_point,
                p_baseline_pheno = p_val,
                n = nrow(pgs_baseline_pheno),
                est_rel_var = est_i$relative_variance,
                est_rel_r2 = est_i$relative_R2,
                r2_baseline_low_ci = r2_base_ci[1],
                r2_baseline_high_ci = r2_base_ci[2],
                r2_masked_low_ci = r2_masked_ci[1],
                r2_masked_high_ci = r2_masked_ci[2],
                rel_var_low_ci = rel_var_ci[1],
                rel_var_high_ci = rel_var_ci[2],
                cor_baseline_masked_low_ci = cor_base_masked_ci[1],
                cor_baseline_masked_high_ci = cor_base_masked_ci[2],
                cor_baseline_masked_sq_low_ci = cor_base_masked_sq_ci[1],
                cor_baseline_masked_sq_high_ci = cor_base_masked_sq_ci[2],
                rel_r2_low_ci = rel_r2_ci[1],
                rel_r2_high_ci = rel_r2_ci[2]
            )
        } # End of param loop
        
        # The parallel worker returns the list of data.tables for this trait
        return(param_results_list) 
        
    } # End of parallel trait loop

    # --- 4. COMBINE RESULTS (within sequential loop) ---
    # Append the results from this 'pop' (which are already combined from all traits)
    # to the final list.
    if (length(trait_results_list) > 0) {
        final_results_list <- append(final_results_list, trait_results_list)
    }

} # End of sequential pop loop

results_dt <- rbindlist(final_results_list)
results_dt[, pgs_method := ifelse(str_detect(param, 'sbayesrc'), 'SBayesRC', 'pT+clump')]

# Plot the relative R2 between WGS and imputed genotype
ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC' & r2_baseline_pheno > 0.01, ], 
       aes(x = trait, y = obs_rel_r2, colour = trait)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_r2_low_ci, 
                    ymax = rel_r2_high_ci)) +
  facet_wrap(. ~ population, scales = 'free') +
#  coord_fixed() +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = "Trait", 
       y = "Observed relative R2 (95%CI)",
       colour = NULL)
# The masked PGS perform slightly better in EUR, and mostly worse in AFR.

# Plot the relative variance between WGS and imputed genotype
ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC' & r2_baseline_pheno > 0.01, ], 
       aes(x = trait, y = obs_rel_var, colour = trait)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_var_low_ci, 
                    ymax = rel_var_high_ci)) +
  facet_wrap(. ~ population, scales = 'free') +
#  coord_fixed() +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = "Trait", 
       y = "Observed relative variance (95%CI)",
       colour = NULL)
# Masked PGS have substantially lower variance as expected

# Plot the squared correlation between WGS and imputed genotype
ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC' & r2_baseline_pheno > 0.01, ], 
       aes(x = trait, y = cor_baseline_masked_sq, colour = trait)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = cor_baseline_masked_sq_low_ci, 
                    ymax = cor_baseline_masked_sq_high_ci)) +
  facet_wrap(. ~ population, scales = 'free') +
#  coord_fixed() +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = "Trait", 
       y = "Squared correlation between PGS (95%CI)",
       colour = NULL)
# There squared correlation between PGS is also significantly below 1.

# Compare squared correlation between PGS and observed relative R2
ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC' & r2_baseline_pheno > 0.01, ], 
       aes(x = cor_baseline_masked_sq, y = obs_rel_r2, colour = trait)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_abline(colour = 'red') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = cor_baseline_masked_sq_low_ci, 
                    xmax = cor_baseline_masked_sq_high_ci)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_r2_low_ci, 
                    ymax = rel_r2_high_ci)) +
  facet_wrap(. ~ population, scales = 'free') +
#  coord_fixed() +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = "Squared correlation between PGS (95%CI)", 
       y = "Observed relative R2 (95%CI)",
       colour = NULL)

# Compare squared correlation between PGS and observed relative R2
ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'pT+clump' & r2_baseline_pheno > 0.01 & grepl('0.01', results_dt$param), ], 
       aes(x = cor_baseline_masked_sq, y = obs_rel_r2, colour = trait)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_abline(colour = 'red') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = cor_baseline_masked_sq_low_ci, 
                    xmax = cor_baseline_masked_sq_high_ci)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_r2_low_ci, 
                    ymax = rel_r2_high_ci)) +
  facet_wrap(. ~ population, scales = 'free') +
#  coord_fixed() +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = "Squared correlation between PGS (95%CI)", 
       y = "Observed relative R2 (95%CI)",
       colour = NULL)

# These results demonstrate that there is some denoising of the PGS occuring when masking by imputation quality.

```

***

## Dense: Baseline vs Masked (proportions)

```{r}

library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(cowplot)
library(stringr)
library(foreach)
library(doMC)
registerDoMC(8)

# --- 1. Data Processing Loop ---
# This section is an enhanced version of your original code.

# Define analysis parameters
populations <- c('EUR')
traits <- c('HT', 'HB', 'PLT', 'BMI', 'BWT', 'MCHC', 'SBP', 'NEU', 'HDL', 'TC')
base_dir_baseline <- '~/pgs_wgs/dense/baseline/'
base_dir_masked <- '~/pgs_wgs/dense/masked/'
pheno_dir <- '/mnt/project/mind_the_gap/phenotype/'
props <- c(0.5, 0.25, 0.05)

n_boot <- 100 # Number of bootstrap replicates (1000 is good, 100 for testing)
set.seed(123)  # For reproducible results

# Create the list to hold all results
final_results_list <- list()

# --- 2. RUN SEQUENTIAL OUTER LOOP (for pop) ---
# We use a standard 'for' loop for the sequential part
for (pop in populations) {
    print(paste("Processing Population:", pop))
  for (prop in props) {
    print(paste("Processing prop:", prop))
    # --- This code runs sequentially on the main thread, once per pop ---
    baseline_file <- paste0(base_dir_baseline, 'ukb.', pop, '.dense.sscore')
    masked_file <- paste0(base_dir_masked, 'ukb.', pop, '.dense.prop_', prop, '.sscore')
    
    if (!file.exists(baseline_file) || !file.exists(masked_file)) {
        print(paste("Skipping pop:", pop, "- files not found"))
        next # Use 'next' to skip to the next 'pop' in a 'for' loop
    }
    
    pgs_baseline <- fread(baseline_file)
    pgs_masked <- fread(masked_file)

    pgs_baseline <- pgs_baseline[pgs_baseline$IID %in% pgs_masked$IID, ]
    pgs_masked <- pgs_masked[pgs_masked$IID %in% pgs_baseline$IID, ]
    # --- End of sequential loading part ---


    # --- 3. RUN PARALLEL INNER LOOP (for trait) ---
    # Now, call foreach %dopar% to process all traits for THIS pop in parallel.
    # The '.export' is crucial to send the loaded data to the workers.
    trait_results_list <- foreach(
        trait = traits,
        .combine = 'append', # Appends the lists returned by each 'trait'
        .packages = 'data.table',
        .export = c('pheno_dir', 'n_boot', 'pgs_baseline', 'pgs_masked', 'pop') 
    ) %dopar% {
        
        pheno_file <- paste0(pheno_dir, trait, '.', pop, '.txt')
        if (!file.exists(pheno_file)) {
            return(list()) # Skip this trait, return an empty list
        }
        
        pheno <- fread(pheno_file)

        # Subset PGS data
        pgs_baseline_i <- pgs_baseline[, c('IID', names(pgs_baseline)[grepl('SUM', names(pgs_baseline)) &
                                                                        grepl(trait, names(pgs_baseline))]), with = F]
        pgs_masked_i <- pgs_masked[, c('IID', names(pgs_masked)[grepl('SUM', names(pgs_masked)) &
                                                                    grepl(trait, names(pgs_masked))]), with = F]
        
        # Merge PGS data
        pgs_baseline_pheno <- merge(pheno, pgs_baseline_i, by = 'IID', sort = FALSE)
        pgs_masked_pheno <- merge(pheno, pgs_masked_i, by = 'IID', sort = FALSE)
        
        param_cols <- names(pgs_baseline_i)[-1]
        
        # This list will hold results for all 'param's for THIS trait
        param_results_list <- list() 

        for (param in param_cols) {
            if (!(param %in% names(pgs_masked_pheno))) next
            
            n <- nrow(pgs_baseline_pheno)
            if (n == 0) next # Safety check
            
            # --- 1.5. BOOTSTRAPPING SECTION ---
            boot_stats <- replicate(n_boot, {
                indices <- sample(1:n, n, replace = TRUE)
                
                boot_base <- pgs_baseline_pheno[indices]
                boot_masked <- pgs_masked_pheno[indices]
                
                pheno_vals_base <- boot_base[[trait]]
                pgs_vals_base <- boot_base[[param]]
                
                pheno_vals_masked <- boot_masked[[trait]]
                pgs_vals_masked <- boot_masked[[param]]
                
                # R2 stats
                r2_base_boot <- tryCatch(cor(pheno_vals_base, pgs_vals_base)^2, error = function(e) NA)
                r2_masked_boot <- tryCatch(cor(pheno_vals_masked, pgs_vals_masked)^2, error = function(e) NA)
                
                # Relative Variance
                var_base_boot <- tryCatch(var(pgs_vals_base), error = function(e) NA)
                var_masked_boot <- tryCatch(var(pgs_vals_masked), error = function(e) NA)
                rel_var_boot <- if (is.na(var_base_boot) || is.na(var_masked_boot) || var_base_boot == 0) {
                    NA
                } else {
                    var_masked_boot / var_base_boot
                }
                
                # Correlation stat
                cor_base_masked_boot <- tryCatch(cor(pgs_vals_base, pgs_vals_masked), error = function(e) NA)
                cor_base_masked_sq_boot <- cor_base_masked_boot^2
                
                # Relative R2
                rel_r2_boot <- if (is.na(r2_base_boot) || is.na(r2_masked_boot) || r2_base_boot == 0) {
                    NA
                } else {
                    r2_masked_boot / r2_base_boot
                }
                
                # Return a named vector
                c(
                    r2_base = r2_base_boot,
                    r2_masked = r2_masked_boot,
                    rel_var = rel_var_boot,
                    cor_base_masked = cor_base_masked_boot,
                    cor_base_masked_sq = cor_base_masked_sq_boot,
                    rel_r2 = rel_r2_boot
                )
            })
            
            # Transpose boot_stats
            boot_stats_t <- t(boot_stats)
            
            # --- Calculate 95% CIs ---
            safe_quantile <- function(col_name) {
                quantile(boot_stats_t[, col_name], c(0.025, 0.975), na.rm = TRUE)
            }
            
            r2_base_ci <- safe_quantile("r2_base")
            r2_masked_ci <- safe_quantile("r2_masked")
            rel_var_ci <- safe_quantile("rel_var")
            cor_base_masked_ci <- safe_quantile("cor_base_masked")
            cor_base_masked_sq_ci <- safe_quantile("cor_base_masked_sq")
            rel_r2_ci <- safe_quantile("rel_r2")
            # --- END BOOTSTRAPPING ---
            
            # --- 1.6. Calculate Point Estimates ---
            r2_baseline_pheno <- cor(pgs_baseline_pheno[[trait]], pgs_baseline_pheno[[param]])^2
            r2_masked_pheno <- cor(pgs_masked_pheno[[trait]], pgs_masked_pheno[[param]])^2
            p_val <- coef(summary(lm(pgs_baseline_pheno[[trait]] ~ pgs_baseline_pheno[[param]])))[2,4]
            cor_base_masked <- cor(pgs_baseline_pheno[[param]], pgs_masked_pheno[[param]])
            cor_baseline_masked_sq <- cor_base_masked^2
            
            var_baseline <- var(pgs_baseline_pheno[[param]])
            var_masked <- var(pgs_masked_pheno[[param]])
            rel_var_point <- var_masked / var_baseline

            # --- 1.7. Store results ---
            param_results_list[[length(param_results_list) + 1]] <- data.table(
                param = param,
                trait = trait,
                population = pop,
                prop = prop,
                r2_baseline_pheno = r2_baseline_pheno,
                r2_masked_pheno = r2_masked_pheno,
                cor_baseline_masked = cor_base_masked,
                cor_baseline_masked_sq = cor_baseline_masked_sq,
                obs_rel_r2 = r2_masked_pheno / r2_baseline_pheno,
                obs_rel_var = rel_var_point,
                p_baseline_pheno = p_val,
                n = nrow(pgs_baseline_pheno),
                r2_baseline_low_ci = r2_base_ci[1],
                r2_baseline_high_ci = r2_base_ci[2],
                r2_masked_low_ci = r2_masked_ci[1],
                r2_masked_high_ci = r2_masked_ci[2],
                rel_var_low_ci = rel_var_ci[1],
                rel_var_high_ci = rel_var_ci[2],
                cor_baseline_masked_low_ci = cor_base_masked_ci[1],
                cor_baseline_masked_high_ci = cor_base_masked_ci[2],
                cor_baseline_masked_sq_low_ci = cor_base_masked_sq_ci[1],
                cor_baseline_masked_sq_high_ci = cor_base_masked_sq_ci[2],
                rel_r2_low_ci = rel_r2_ci[1],
                rel_r2_high_ci = rel_r2_ci[2]
            )
        } # End of param loop
        
        # The parallel worker returns the list of data.tables for this trait
        return(param_results_list) 
        
    } # End of parallel trait loop

    # --- 4. COMBINE RESULTS (within sequential loop) ---
    # Append the results from this 'pop' (which are already combined from all traits)
    # to the final list.
    if (length(trait_results_list) > 0) {
        final_results_list <- append(final_results_list, trait_results_list)
    }
  }

} # End of sequential pop loop

results_dt <- rbindlist(final_results_list)
results_dt[, pgs_method := ifelse(str_detect(param, 'sbayesrc'), 'SBayesRC', 'pT+clump')]
results_dt$call_rate<-paste0('Call rate = ', 1-results_dt$prop)

# Plot the relative R2 between WGS and imputed genotype
ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC' & r2_baseline_pheno > 0.01, ], 
       aes(x = trait, y = obs_rel_r2, colour = trait)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_r2_low_ci, 
                    ymax = rel_r2_high_ci)) +
  facet_grid(. ~ call_rate) +
#  coord_fixed() +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = "Trait", 
       y = "Observed relative R2 (95%CI)",
       colour = NULL)
# The masked PGS perform slightly better in EUR, and mostly worse in AFR.

# Plot the relative variance between WGS and imputed genotype
ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC' & r2_baseline_pheno > 0.01, ], 
       aes(x = trait, y = obs_rel_var, colour = trait)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_var_low_ci, 
                    ymax = rel_var_high_ci)) +
  facet_grid(. ~ call_rate) +
#  coord_fixed() +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = "Trait", 
       y = "Observed relative variance (95%CI)",
       colour = NULL)
# Masked PGS have substantially lower variance as expected

# Plot the squared correlation between WGS and imputed genotype
ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC' & r2_baseline_pheno > 0.01, ], 
       aes(x = trait, y = cor_baseline_masked_sq, colour = trait)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = cor_baseline_masked_sq_low_ci, 
                    ymax = cor_baseline_masked_sq_high_ci)) +
  facet_grid(. ~ call_rate) +
#  coord_fixed() +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = "Trait", 
       y = "Squared correlation between PGS (95%CI)",
       colour = NULL)
# There squared correlation between PGS is also significantly below 1.

# Compare squared correlation between PGS and observed relative R2
ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC' & r2_baseline_pheno > 0.01, ], 
       aes(x = cor_baseline_masked_sq, y = obs_rel_r2, colour = trait)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_abline(colour = 'red') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = cor_baseline_masked_sq_low_ci, 
                    xmax = cor_baseline_masked_sq_high_ci)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_r2_low_ci, 
                    ymax = rel_r2_high_ci)) +
  facet_grid(. ~ call_rate) +
#  coord_fixed() +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = "Squared correlation between PGS (95%CI)", 
       y = "Observed relative R2 (95%CI)",
       colour = NULL)

# Compare squared correlation between PGS and observed relative R2
ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'pT+clump' & r2_baseline_pheno > 0.01 & grepl('0.01', results_dt$param), ], 
       aes(x = cor_baseline_masked_sq, y = obs_rel_r2, colour = trait)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_abline(colour = 'red') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = cor_baseline_masked_sq_low_ci, 
                    xmax = cor_baseline_masked_sq_high_ci)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_r2_low_ci, 
                    ymax = rel_r2_high_ci)) +
  facet_grid(. ~ call_rate) +
#  coord_fixed() +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = "Squared correlation between PGS (95%CI)", 
       y = "Observed relative R2 (95%CI)",
       colour = NULL)

# These results demonstrate that there is some denoising of the PGS occuring when masking by imputation quality.

```

***

## WGS Sparse: Baseline vs Masked (Random)

```{bash}
tar -xvf /mnt/project/mind_the_gap/pgs_wgs.tar
```

```{r}
library(data.table)
library(scales)
library(ggplot2)
library(cowplot)
library(stringr)
library(foreach)
library(doMC)
registerDoMC(6)

# --- 1. Data Processing Loop ---
# Define analysis parameters
populations <- c('EUR', 'AFR')
traits <- c('HT', 'HB', 'PLT', 'BMI', 'BWT', 'MCHC', 'SBP', 'NEU', 'HDL', 'TC')
base_dir_baseline <- '~/pgs_wgs/wgs_sparse/baseline/'
base_dir_masked <- '~/pgs_wgs/wgs_sparse/masked/'
pheno_dir <- '/mnt/project/mind_the_gap/phenotype/'
props <- c(0.05, 0.25, 0.5)

# Read in the estimated relative r2 and variance
est_dat <- NULL
for(i in props){
  tmp <- fread(paste0('/mnt/project/mind_the_gap/score_files/est_rsq.wgs.sparse.random.',i,'.txt'))
  tmp$prop <- i
  est_dat <- rbind(est_dat, tmp)
}
est_dat<-est_dat[, c('population', 'beta_set','relative_variance','relative_R2','prop'), with = F]
names(est_dat)<-c('population', 'param','est_rel_var', 'est_rel_r2', 'prop')
est_dat$param<-paste0(est_dat$param, '_SUM')

n_boot <- 1000 # Number of bootstrap replicates
set.seed(123)  # For reproducible results

# Create the list to hold all results
final_results_list <- list()

# --- 2. RUN SEQUENTIAL OUTER LOOP (for pop) ---
for (pop in populations) {
    print(paste("Processing Population:", pop))
  for (prop in props) {
    print(paste("Processing prop:", prop))
    
    baseline_file <- paste0(base_dir_baseline, 'ukb.', pop, '.sparse.sscore')
    masked_file <- paste0(base_dir_masked, 'ukb.', pop, '.sparse.random.prop_', prop, '.sscore')
    
    if (!file.exists(baseline_file) || !file.exists(masked_file)) {
        print(paste("Skipping pop:", pop, "- files not found"))
        next 
    }
    
    pgs_baseline <- fread(baseline_file)
    pgs_masked <- fread(masked_file)

    pgs_baseline <- pgs_baseline[pgs_baseline$IID %in% pgs_masked$IID, ]
    pgs_masked <- pgs_masked[pgs_masked$IID %in% pgs_baseline$IID, ]

# --- 3. RUN PARALLEL INNER LOOP (for trait) ---
    trait_results_list <- foreach(
        trait = traits,
        .combine = 'append', 
        .packages = 'data.table',
        .export = c('pheno_dir', 'n_boot', 'pgs_baseline', 'pgs_masked', 'pop', 'est_dat', 'prop') 
    ) %dopar% {
        
        pheno_file <- paste0(pheno_dir, trait, '.', pop, '.txt')
        if (!file.exists(pheno_file)) {
            return(list()) 
        }
        
        pheno <- fread(pheno_file)

        # Subset PGS data
        pgs_baseline_i <- pgs_baseline[, c('IID', names(pgs_baseline)[grepl('SUM', names(pgs_baseline)) &
                                                                        grepl(trait, names(pgs_baseline))]), with = F]
        pgs_masked_i <- pgs_masked[, c('IID', names(pgs_masked)[grepl('SUM', names(pgs_masked)) &
                                                                    grepl(trait, names(pgs_masked))]), with = F]
        
        # Merge PGS data
        pgs_baseline_pheno <- merge(pheno, pgs_baseline_i, by = 'IID', sort = FALSE)
        pgs_masked_pheno <- merge(pheno, pgs_masked_i, by = 'IID', sort = FALSE)
        
        # --- CRITICAL ALIGNMENT STEP ---
        common_iids <- intersect(pgs_baseline_pheno$IID, pgs_masked_pheno$IID)
        pgs_baseline_pheno <- pgs_baseline_pheno[IID %in% common_iids][order(IID)]
        pgs_masked_pheno <- pgs_masked_pheno[IID %in% common_iids][order(IID)]
        
        param_cols <- names(pgs_baseline_i)[-1]
        
        param_results_list <- list() 

        for (param in param_cols) {
            if (!(param %in% names(pgs_masked_pheno))) next
            
            # --- Get estimated values for this param and prop ---
            current_param_val <- param
            current_prop_val <- prop # 'prop' is available from the .export
            current_est_row <- est_dat[population == pop & param == current_param_val & prop == current_prop_val,]
            if (nrow(current_est_row) == 0) {
                print(paste("Skipping param:", param, "for prop:", prop, "- No estimated values found"))
                next
            }
            est_rel_var_point <- current_est_row$est_rel_var
            est_rel_r2_point <- current_est_row$est_rel_r2
            
            n <- nrow(pgs_baseline_pheno)
            if (n == 0) next 

            # --- OPTIMIZATION 2: Get vectors *before* the loop ---
            pheno_full_vec <- pgs_baseline_pheno[['outcome']]
            pgs_base_full_vec <- pgs_baseline_pheno[[param]]
            pgs_masked_full_vec <- pgs_masked_pheno[[param]]
            
            # --- 1.5. BOOTSTRAPPING SECTION ---
            boot_stats <- replicate(n_boot, {
                indices <- sample(1:n, n, replace = TRUE)
                
                # --- OPTIMIZATION 2: Subset vectors (much faster) ---
                pheno_vals_boot <- pheno_full_vec[indices]
                pgs_vals_base <- pgs_base_full_vec[indices]
                pgs_vals_masked <- pgs_masked_full_vec[indices]
                
                # R2 stats
                r2_baseline_boot <- tryCatch(cor(pheno_vals_boot, pgs_vals_base)^2, error = function(e) NA)
                r2_masked_boot <- tryCatch(cor(pheno_vals_boot, pgs_vals_masked)^2, error = function(e) NA)
                
                # Relative Variance
                var_baseline_boot <- tryCatch(var(pgs_vals_base), error = function(e) NA)
                var_masked_boot <- tryCatch(var(pgs_vals_masked), error = function(e) NA)
                rel_var_boot <- if (is.na(var_baseline_boot) || is.na(var_masked_boot) || var_baseline_boot == 0) {
                    NA
                } else {
                    var_masked_boot / var_baseline_boot
                }
                
                # Correlation stat
                cor_baseline_masked_boot <- tryCatch(cor(pgs_vals_base, pgs_vals_masked), error = function(e) NA)
                cor_baseline_masked_sq_boot <- cor_baseline_masked_boot^2
                
                # Relative R2
                rel_r2_boot <- if (is.na(r2_baseline_boot) || is.na(r2_masked_boot) || r2_baseline_boot == 0) {
                    NA
                } else {
                    r2_masked_boot / r2_baseline_boot
                }
                
                # --- OPTIMIZATION 1: SLOPE CALCULATIONS (Replaced lm()) ---
                beta_true_boot <- tryCatch(cov(pheno_vals_boot, pgs_vals_base) / var_baseline_boot, error = function(e) NA)
                beta_naive_boot <- tryCatch(cov(pheno_vals_boot, pgs_vals_masked) / var_masked_boot, error = function(e) NA)

                # --- 1. "Observed" Correction ---
                attenuation_factor_obs_boot <- if (is.na(cor_baseline_masked_sq_boot) || is.na(rel_var_boot) || rel_var_boot == 0) {
                    NA
                } else {
                    sqrt(cor_baseline_masked_sq_boot / rel_var_boot)
                }
                beta_corrected_obs_boot <- if (is.na(beta_naive_boot) || is.na(attenuation_factor_obs_boot) || attenuation_factor_obs_boot == 0) {
                    NA
                } else {
                    beta_naive_boot / attenuation_factor_obs_boot
                }

                # --- 2. "Estimated" Correction ---
                attenuation_factor_est_boot <- if (is.na(est_rel_r2_point) || is.na(est_rel_var_point) || est_rel_var_point == 0) {
                    NA
                } else {
                    sqrt(est_rel_r2_point / est_rel_var_point)
                }
                beta_corrected_est_boot <- if (is.na(beta_naive_boot) || is.na(attenuation_factor_est_boot) || attenuation_factor_est_boot == 0) {
                    NA
                } else {
                    beta_naive_boot / attenuation_factor_est_boot
                }

                # --- Ratios ---
                beta_naive_ratio_boot <- if (is.na(beta_naive_boot) || is.na(beta_true_boot) || beta_true_boot == 0) {
                    NA
                } else {
                    beta_naive_boot / beta_true_boot
                }
                beta_corrected_obs_ratio_boot <- if (is.na(beta_corrected_obs_boot) || is.na(beta_true_boot) || beta_true_boot == 0) {
                    NA
                } else {
                    beta_corrected_obs_boot / beta_true_boot
                }
                beta_corrected_est_ratio_boot <- if (is.na(beta_corrected_est_boot) || is.na(beta_true_boot) || beta_true_boot == 0) {
                    NA
                } else {
                    beta_corrected_est_boot / beta_true_boot
                }
                
                # Return a named vector
                c(
                    r2_baseline = r2_baseline_boot,
                    r2_masked = r2_masked_boot,
                    rel_var = rel_var_boot,
                    cor_baseline_masked = cor_baseline_masked_boot,
                    cor_baseline_masked_sq = cor_baseline_masked_sq_boot,
                    rel_r2 = rel_r2_boot,
                    beta_true = beta_true_boot,
                    beta_naive = beta_naive_boot,
                    beta_corrected_obs = beta_corrected_obs_boot,
                    beta_corrected_est = beta_corrected_est_boot,
                    beta_naive_ratio = beta_naive_ratio_boot,
                    beta_corrected_obs_ratio = beta_corrected_obs_ratio_boot,
                    beta_corrected_est_ratio = beta_corrected_est_ratio_boot
                )
            })
            
            # Transpose boot_stats
            boot_stats_t <- t(boot_stats)
            
            # --- Calculate 95% CIs ---
            safe_quantile <- function(col_name) {
                if (!col_name %in% colnames(boot_stats_t) || all(is.na(boot_stats_t[, col_name]))) {
                    return(c(NA, NA))
                }
                quantile(boot_stats_t[, col_name], c(0.025, 0.975), na.rm = TRUE)
            }
            
            r2_baseline_ci <- safe_quantile("r2_baseline")
            r2_masked_ci <- safe_quantile("r2_masked")
            rel_var_ci <- safe_quantile("rel_var")
            cor_baseline_masked_ci <- safe_quantile("cor_baseline_masked")
            cor_baseline_masked_sq_ci <- safe_quantile("cor_baseline_masked_sq")
            rel_r2_ci <- safe_quantile("rel_r2")
            
            beta_true_ci <- safe_quantile("beta_true")
            beta_naive_ci <- safe_quantile("beta_naive")
            beta_corrected_obs_ci <- safe_quantile("beta_corrected_obs")
            beta_corrected_est_ci <- safe_quantile("beta_corrected_est")
            beta_naive_ratio_ci <- safe_quantile("beta_naive_ratio")
            beta_corrected_obs_ratio_ci <- safe_quantile("beta_corrected_obs_ratio")
            beta_corrected_est_ratio_ci <- safe_quantile("beta_corrected_est_ratio")
            # --- END BOOTSTRAPPING ---
            
            # --- 1.6. Calculate Point Estimates ---
            pheno_point <- pgs_baseline_pheno[['outcome']]
            pgs_s_point <- pgs_baseline_pheno[[param]]
            pgs_s_prime_point <- pgs_masked_pheno[[param]]
            
            r2_baseline_pheno <- cor(pheno_point, pgs_s_point)^2
            r2_masked_pheno <- cor(pheno_point, pgs_s_prime_point)^2
            # This lm() is fine, it only runs once
            p_val <- coef(summary(lm(pheno_point ~ pgs_s_point)))[2,4] 
            cor_baseline_masked <- cor(pgs_s_point, pgs_s_prime_point)
            cor_baseline_masked_sq <- cor_baseline_masked^2
            
            var_baseline <- var(pgs_s_point)
            var_masked <- var(pgs_s_prime_point)
            rel_var_point <- var_masked / var_baseline

            # --- OPTIMIZATION 1: Slope Point Estimates (Replaced lm()) ---
            beta_true_point <- cov(pheno_point, pgs_s_point) / var_baseline
            beta_naive_point <- cov(pheno_point, pgs_s_prime_point) / var_masked
            
            # "Observed" Correction
            attenuation_factor_obs_point <- if (rel_var_point == 0) NA else sqrt(cor_baseline_masked_sq / rel_var_point)
            beta_corrected_obs_point <- if (is.na(attenuation_factor_obs_point) || attenuation_factor_obs_point == 0) NA else (beta_naive_point / attenuation_factor_obs_point)
            
            # "Estimated" Correction
            attenuation_factor_est_point <- if (est_rel_var_point == 0) NA else sqrt(est_rel_r2_point / est_rel_var_point)
            beta_corrected_est_point <- if (is.na(attenuation_factor_est_point) || attenuation_factor_est_point == 0) NA else (beta_naive_point / attenuation_factor_est_point)

            # Ratios
            beta_naive_ratio_point <- if (beta_true_point == 0) NA else (beta_naive_point / beta_true_point)
            beta_corrected_obs_ratio_point <- if (beta_true_point == 0) NA else (beta_corrected_obs_point / beta_true_point)
            beta_corrected_est_ratio_point <- if (beta_true_point == 0) NA else (beta_corrected_est_point / beta_true_point)

            # --- 1.7. Store results ---
            param_results_list[[length(param_results_list) + 1]] <- data.table(
                param = param,
                trait = trait,
                population = pop,
                prop = prop,
                r2_baseline_pheno = r2_baseline_pheno,
                r2_masked_pheno = r2_masked_pheno,
                cor_baseline_masked = cor_baseline_masked,
                cor_baseline_masked_sq = cor_baseline_masked_sq,
                obs_rel_r2 = r2_masked_pheno / r2_baseline_pheno,
                obs_rel_var = rel_var_point,
                est_rel_r2 = est_rel_r2_point, 
                est_rel_var = est_rel_var_point, 
                p_baseline_pheno = p_val,
                n = nrow(pgs_baseline_pheno),
                
                r2_baseline_low_ci = r2_baseline_ci[1], r2_baseline_high_ci = r2_baseline_ci[2],
                r2_masked_low_ci = r2_masked_ci[1], r2_masked_high_ci = r2_masked_ci[2],
                rel_var_low_ci = rel_var_ci[1], rel_var_high_ci = rel_var_ci[2],
                cor_baseline_masked_low_ci = cor_baseline_masked_ci[1], cor_baseline_masked_high_ci = cor_baseline_masked_ci[2],
                cor_baseline_masked_sq_low_ci = cor_baseline_masked_sq_ci[1], cor_baseline_masked_sq_high_ci = cor_baseline_masked_sq_ci[2],
                rel_r2_low_ci = rel_r2_ci[1], rel_r2_high_ci = rel_r2_ci[2],
                
                beta_true = beta_true_point,
                beta_true_low_ci = beta_true_ci[1], beta_true_high_ci = beta_true_ci[2],
                beta_naive = beta_naive_point,
                beta_naive_low_ci = beta_naive_ci[1], beta_naive_high_ci = beta_naive_ci[2],
                beta_naive_ratio = beta_naive_ratio_point,
                beta_naive_ratio_low_ci = beta_naive_ratio_ci[1], beta_naive_ratio_high_ci = beta_naive_ratio_ci[2],
                
                beta_corrected_obs = beta_corrected_obs_point,
                beta_corrected_obs_low_ci = beta_corrected_obs_ci[1], beta_corrected_obs_high_ci = beta_corrected_obs_ci[2],
                beta_corrected_obs_ratio = beta_corrected_obs_ratio_point,
                beta_corrected_obs_ratio_low_ci = beta_corrected_obs_ratio_ci[1], beta_corrected_obs_ratio_high_ci = beta_corrected_obs_ratio_ci[2],
                
                beta_corrected_est = beta_corrected_est_point,
                beta_corrected_est_low_ci = beta_corrected_est_ci[1], beta_corrected_est_high_ci = beta_corrected_est_ci[2],
                beta_corrected_est_ratio = beta_corrected_est_ratio_point,
                beta_corrected_est_ratio_low_ci = beta_corrected_est_ratio_ci[1], beta_corrected_est_ratio_high_ci = beta_corrected_est_ratio_ci[2]
            )
        } # End of param loop
        
        return(param_results_list) 
        
    } # End of parallel trait loop

    # --- 4. COMBINE RESULTS (within sequential loop) ---
    if (length(trait_results_list) > 0) {
        final_results_list <- append(final_results_list, trait_results_list)
    }
  } # End of prop loop

} # End of sequential pop loop

results_dt <- rbindlist(final_results_list)
results_dt[, pgs_method := ifelse(str_detect(param, 'sbayesrc'), 'SBayesRC', 'pT+clump')]
results_dt$call_rate<-paste0('Call rate = ', 1-results_dt$prop)

write.csv(results_dt, '~/wgs_random_sparse_results.csv', row.names=F)
system('dx upload ~/wgs_random_sparse_results.csv --path mind_the_gap/results/')
results_dt <- fread('/mnt/project/mind_the_gap/results/wgs_random_sparse_results.csv')

# Plot the relative R2 between baseline and masked PGS
ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC' & results_dt$population == 'EUR', ], 
       aes(x = trait, y = obs_rel_r2, colour = trait)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_r2_low_ci, 
                    ymax = rel_r2_high_ci)) +
  facet_grid(. ~ call_rate) +
#  coord_fixed() +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = "Trait", 
       y = "Observed relative R2 (95%CI)",
       colour = NULL)

# Plot the relative variance between baseline and masked PGS
ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC', ], 
       aes(x = trait, y = obs_rel_var, colour = trait)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_var_low_ci, 
                    ymax = rel_var_high_ci)) +
  facet_grid(. ~ call_rate) +
#  coord_fixed() +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = "Trait", 
       y = "Observed relative variance (95%CI)",
       colour = NULL)
# Masked PGS have substantially lower variance as expected

# Compare observed relative R2 and relative variance
p1 <- ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC' & results_dt$population == 'EUR', ], 
       aes(x = obs_rel_var, y = obs_rel_r2, colour = trait)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_abline(colour = 'red') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = rel_var_low_ci, 
                    xmax = rel_var_high_ci), width = 0) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_r2_low_ci, 
                    ymax = rel_r2_high_ci), width = 0) +
  facet_grid(. ~ call_rate) +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = expression(Observed~Var[s*minute] / Var[s]),
        y = expression(Observed~R[s*minute]^2 / R[s]^2),
        colour = NULL)

# Compare squared correlation between PGS and observed relative R2
p2 <- ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC' & results_dt$population == 'EUR', ], 
       aes(x = cor_baseline_masked_sq, y = obs_rel_r2, colour = trait)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_abline(colour = 'red') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = cor_baseline_masked_sq_low_ci, 
                    xmax = cor_baseline_masked_sq_high_ci), width = 0) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_r2_low_ci, 
                    ymax = rel_r2_high_ci), width = 0) +
  facet_grid(. ~ call_rate) +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = expression(Observed~Cor(s, s*minute)^2),
        y = expression(Observed~R[s*minute]^2 / R[s]^2),
        colour = NULL)

png('~/obs_rel_var_r2_comp.png', res = 300, width = 2750, height = 2500, units = 'px')
plot_grid(p1, p2, labels = c('A', 'B'), ncol = 1)
dev.off()
system('dx upload ~/obs_rel_var_r2_comp.png --path mind_the_gap/results/')

# Compare squared correlation between PGS and observed relative R2
p1 <- ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC' & results_dt$r2_baseline_pheno > 0.01 & results_dt$population == 'AFR', ], 
       aes(x = cor_baseline_masked_sq, y = obs_rel_r2, colour = trait)) +

  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = cor_baseline_masked_sq_low_ci, 
                    xmax = cor_baseline_masked_sq_high_ci), width = 0) +

                    ymax = rel_r2_high_ci), width = 0) +

  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = expression(Observed~Cor(s, s*minute)^2),
        y = expression(Observed~R[s*minute]^2 / R[s]^2),
        colour = NULL)

p2 <- ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'pT+clump' & results_dt$r2_baseline_pheno > 0.01 & results_dt$population == 'AFR' & grepl('0_1_', results_dt$param), ], 
       aes(x = cor_baseline_masked_sq, y = obs_rel_r2, colour = trait)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_abline(colour = 'red') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = cor_baseline_masked_sq_low_ci, 
                    xmax = cor_baseline_masked_sq_high_ci), width = 0) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_r2_low_ci, 
                    ymax = rel_r2_high_ci), width = 0) +
  facet_grid(. ~ call_rate) +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +

        y = expression(Observed~R[s*minute]^2 / R[s]^2),
        colour = NULL)

# Add titles to p1 and p2
p1 <- p1 + labs(title = 'SBayesRC: Random masking')
p2 <- p2 + labs(title = 'pT+clump: Random masking')

png('~/obs_rel_var_r2_comp_afr.png', res = 300, width = 2750, height = 2500, units = 'px')
plot_grid(p2, p1, labels = c('A', 'B'), ncol = 1)
dev.off()
system('dx upload ~/obs_rel_var_r2_comp_afr.png --path mind_the_gap/results/')

# Compare squared correlation between PGS and observed relative R2
ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'pT+clump' & population == 'EUR' & grepl('0_1_', results_dt$param), ], 
       aes(x = cor_baseline_masked_sq, y = obs_rel_r2, colour = trait)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_abline(colour = 'red') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = cor_baseline_masked_sq_low_ci, 
                    xmax = cor_baseline_masked_sq_high_ci), width = 0) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_r2_low_ci, 
                    ymax = rel_r2_high_ci), width = 0) +
  facet_grid(. ~ call_rate) +
#  coord_fixed() +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = "Squared correlation between PGS (95%CI)", 
       y = "Observed relative R2 (95%CI)",
       colour = NULL)

# Plot the PGS slope with phenotype using baseline, masked PGS, and masked PGS after correction based on relative var and cor(s,s')^2
results_dt$call_rate <- factor(results_dt$call_rate, levels = c("Call rate = 0.5", "Call rate = 0.75", "Call rate = 0.95"))

# Reshape the data from wide to long format
plot_data <- melt(results_dt,
     id.vars = c("trait", "population", "pgs_method", "call_rate"),
     measure.vars = list(
         slope_ratio = c("beta_naive_ratio", "beta_corrected_obs_ratio"),
         low_ci = c("beta_naive_ratio_low_ci", "beta_corrected_obs_ratio_low_ci"),
         high_ci = c("beta_naive_ratio_high_ci", "beta_corrected_obs_ratio_high_ci")
     ),
     variable.name = "model_type_index",
     value.name = c("slope_ratio", "low_ci", "high_ci")
)

# Map the numeric index to a clean factor name
plot_data[, model_type := factor(plot_data$model_type_index,  labels = c("Naive", "Corrected"))]

# --- 3. Create the Plot ---
plot_slope_comparison <- ggplot(
  plot_data[plot_data$pgs_method == 'SBayesRC' & plot_data$population == 'EUR',], 
  aes(x = slope_ratio, 
      y = trait, 
      color = model_type)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1.0, color = "black", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = low_ci, xmax = high_ci), 
                 height = 0, 
                 position = position_dodge(width = 0.6),
                 alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, position = position_dodge(width = 0.6)) +
  facet_grid(trait~ call_rate, scales = 'free_y') +
  labs(
      x = "Slope Ratio (Estimated Slope / True Slope)",
      y = "Trait",
      color = "Model Type"
  ) +
  xlim(c(0.90,NA)) +
  theme_half_open() +
  background_grid(major = "x") +
  panel_border() +
  theme(strip.text.y = element_blank())

png('~/recalibrate_sparse_wgs.png', res = 300, width = 2750, height = 1750, units = 'px')
plot_slope_comparison
dev.off()
system('dx upload ~/recalibrate_sparse_wgs.png --path mind_the_gap/results/')

#####
# Compare to estimated relative variance and R2
#####

# Compare estimated and observed relative R2
p1 <- ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC' & results_dt$population == 'EUR', ], 
       aes(x = est_rel_r2, y = obs_rel_r2, colour = trait)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_abline(colour = 'red') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_r2_low_ci, 
                    ymax = rel_r2_high_ci), width = 0) +
  facet_grid(. ~ call_rate) +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(), limits = c(NA, 1)) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(), limits = c(NA, 1)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = expression(Estimated~Cor(s, s*minute)^2),
        y = expression(Observed~R[s*minute]^2 / R[s]^2),
        colour = NULL)

# Compare estimated and observed relative R2
p2 <- ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC' & results_dt$population == 'EUR', ], 
       aes(x = est_rel_r2, y = cor_baseline_masked_sq, colour = trait)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_abline(colour = 'red') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = cor_baseline_masked_sq_low_ci, 
                    ymax = cor_baseline_masked_sq_high_ci), width = 0) +
  facet_grid(. ~ call_rate) +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(), limits = c(NA, 1)) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(), limits = c(NA, 1)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = expression(Estimated~Cor(s, s*minute)^2),
        y = expression(Observed~Cor(s, s*minute)^2),
        colour = NULL)

p3 <- ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC' & results_dt$population == 'EUR', ], 
       aes(x = est_rel_var, y = obs_rel_var, colour = trait)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_abline(colour = 'red') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_var_low_ci, 
                    ymax = rel_var_high_ci), width = 0) +
  facet_grid(. ~ call_rate) +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(), limits = c(NA, 1)) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(), limits = c(NA, 1)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = expression(Estimated~Var[s*minute] / Var[s]),
        y = expression(Observed~Var[s*minute] / Var[s]),
        colour = NULL)

png('~/obs_est_rel_var_r2_comp_sparse_sbayesrc.png', res = 300, width = 2750, height = 3850, units = 'px')
plot_grid(p1, p2, p3, labels = c('A', 'B', 'C'), ncol = 1)
dev.off()
system('dx upload ~/obs_est_rel_var_r2_comp_sparse_sbayesrc.png --path mind_the_gap/results/')

png('~/obs_est_rel_var_r2_comp_sparse_sbayesrc_small.png', res = 300, width = 2750, height = 2500, units = 'px')
plot_grid(p2, p3, labels = c('A', 'B'), ncol = 1)
dev.off()
system('dx upload ~/obs_est_rel_var_r2_comp_sparse_sbayesrc_small.png --path mind_the_gap/results/')

#### pT+clump
# Compare estimated and observed relative R2
p1 <- ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'pT+clump' & results_dt$population == 'EUR' & grepl('0_1_', results_dt$param), ], 
       aes(x = est_rel_r2, y = obs_rel_r2, colour = trait)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_abline(colour = 'red') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_r2_low_ci, 
                    ymax = rel_r2_high_ci), width = 0) +
  facet_grid(. ~ call_rate) +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(), limits = c(NA, 1)) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(), limits = c(NA, 1)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = expression(Estimated~Cor(s, s*minute)^2),
        y = expression(Observed~R[s*minute]^2 / R[s]^2),
        colour = NULL)

# Compare estimated and observed relative R2
p2 <- ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'pT+clump' & results_dt$population == 'EUR' & grepl('0_1_', results_dt$param), ], 
       aes(x = est_rel_r2, y = cor_baseline_masked_sq, colour = trait)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_abline(colour = 'red') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = cor_baseline_masked_sq_low_ci, 
                    ymax = cor_baseline_masked_sq_high_ci), width = 0) +
  facet_grid(. ~ call_rate) +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(), limits = c(NA, 1)) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(), limits = c(NA, 1)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = expression(Estimated~Cor(s, s*minute)^2),
        y = expression(Observed~Cor(s, s*minute)^2),
        colour = NULL)

p3 <- ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'pT+clump' & results_dt$population == 'EUR' & grepl('0_1_', results_dt$param), ], 
       aes(x = est_rel_var, y = obs_rel_var, colour = trait)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_abline(colour = 'red') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_var_low_ci, 
                    ymax = rel_var_high_ci), width = 0) +
  facet_grid(. ~ call_rate) +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(), limits = c(NA, 1)) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(), limits = c(NA, 1)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = expression(Estimated~Var[s*minute] / Var[s]),
        y = expression(Observed~Var[s*minute] / Var[s]),
        colour = NULL)

# Plot the PGS slope with phenotype using baseline, masked PGS, and masked PGS after correction based on relative var and cor(s,s')^2, and the estimated relative r2 and variance
results_dt$call_rate <- factor(results_dt$call_rate, levels = c("Call rate = 0.5", "Call rate = 0.75", "Call rate = 0.95"))

# Reshape the data from wide to long format
plot_data <- melt(results_dt,
     id.vars = c("trait", "population", "pgs_method", "call_rate"),
     measure.vars = list(
         slope_ratio = c("beta_naive_ratio", "beta_corrected_obs_ratio", "beta_corrected_est_ratio"),
         low_ci = c("beta_naive_ratio_low_ci", "beta_corrected_obs_ratio_low_ci", "beta_corrected_est_ratio_low_ci"),
         high_ci = c("beta_naive_ratio_high_ci", "beta_corrected_obs_ratio_high_ci", "beta_corrected_est_ratio_high_ci")
     ),
     variable.name = "model_type_index",
     value.name = c("slope_ratio", "low_ci", "high_ci")
)

# Map the numeric index to a clean factor name
plot_data[, model_type := factor(plot_data$model_type_index,  labels = c("Naive", "Corrected (observed)", "Corrected (estimated)"))]

# --- 3. Create the Plot ---
plot_slope_comparison <- ggplot(
  plot_data[plot_data$pgs_method == 'SBayesRC' & plot_data$population == 'EUR',], 
  aes(x = slope_ratio, 
      y = trait, 
      color = model_type)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1.0, color = "black", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = low_ci, xmax = high_ci), 
                 height = 0, 
                 position = position_dodge(width = 0.6),
                 alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_point(size = 2.5, position = position_dodge(width = 0.6)) +
  facet_grid(trait~ call_rate, scales = 'free_y') +
  labs(
      x = "Slope Ratio (Estimated Slope / True Slope)",
      y = "Trait",
      color = "Model Type"
  ) +
  xlim(c(0.90,NA)) +
  theme_half_open() +
  background_grid(major = "x") +
  panel_border() +
  theme(strip.text.y = element_blank())

png('~/recalibrate_sparse_wgs_with_estimated.png', res = 300, width = 2750, height = 1750, units = 'px')
plot_slope_comparison
dev.off()
system('dx upload ~/recalibrate_sparse_wgs_with_estimated.png --path mind_the_gap/results/')

#####
# Compare to estimated relative variance and R2 (AFR)
#####

# Compare estimated and observed relative R2
p1 <- ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC' & results_dt$population == 'AFR', ], 
       aes(x = est_rel_r2, y = obs_rel_r2, colour = trait)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_abline(colour = 'red') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_r2_low_ci, 
                    ymax = rel_r2_high_ci), width = 0) +
  facet_grid(. ~ call_rate) +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +   # ← no limits here
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +   # ← no limits here
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(NA, 1), ylim = c(NA, 1)) +  # limits here 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = expression(Estimated~Cor(s, s*minute)^2),
        y = expression(Observed~R[s*minute]^2 / R[s]^2),
        colour = NULL)

# Compare estimated and observed relative R2
p2 <- ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC' & results_dt$population == 'AFR', ], 
       aes(x = est_rel_r2, y = cor_baseline_masked_sq, colour = trait)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_abline(colour = 'red') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = cor_baseline_masked_sq_low_ci, 
                    ymax = cor_baseline_masked_sq_high_ci), width = 0) +
  facet_grid(. ~ call_rate) +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +   # ← no limits here
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +   # ← no limits here
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(NA, 1), ylim = c(NA, 1)) +  # limits here 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = expression(Estimated~Cor(s, s*minute)^2),
        y = expression(Observed~Cor(s, s*minute)^2),
        colour = NULL)

p3 <- ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC' & results_dt$population == 'AFR', ], 
       aes(x = est_rel_var, y = obs_rel_var, colour = trait)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_abline(colour = 'red') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_var_low_ci, 
                    ymax = rel_var_high_ci), width = 0) +
  facet_grid(. ~ call_rate) +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +   # ← no limits here
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +   # ← no limits here
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(NA, 1), ylim = c(NA, 1)) +  # limits here 
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = expression(Estimated~Var[s*minute] / Var[s]),
        y = expression(Observed~Var[s*minute] / Var[s]),
        colour = NULL)

png('~/obs_est_rel_var_r2_comp_sparse_sbayesrc_afr.png', res = 300, width = 2750, height = 3850, units = 'px')

dev.off()

# Plot recalibration for AFR
plot_slope_comparison <- ggplot(
  plot_data[plot_data$pgs_method == 'SBayesRC' & plot_data$population == 'AFR',], 
  aes(x = slope_ratio, 
      y = trait, 
      color = model_type)) +

  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = low_ci, xmax = high_ci), 
                 height = 0,

```

***

### Make plot illustrating change in PGS percentile

```{r}
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(cowplot) # For theme_half_open()
library(stringr) # For str_detect()
library(dplyr)   # For percent_rank()

# --- 1. Define Example Parameters ---
example_trait <- 'BMI'
example_pop <- 'EUR'
example_method <- 'SBayesRC' # Case-insensitive, 'sbayesrc' also works

# Define paths and proportions
base_dir_baseline <- '~/pgs_wgs/wgs_sparse/baseline/'
base_dir_masked <- '~/pgs_wgs/wgs_sparse/masked/'
props <- c(0.05, 0.25, 0.5)

# --- NEW: Load your main results file ---
# This file contains the obs_rel_var and est_rel_var
results_file <- '~/wgs_random_sparse_results.csv' 
if (!file.exists(results_file)) {
  stop(paste("Main results file not found:", results_file))
}
results_dt <- fread(results_file)

# This list will hold the binned data for each 'prop'
plot_data_list <- list()

print(paste("Generating STANDARDIZED percentile plot for:", example_trait, "in", example_pop))

# --- 2. Find the correct column name ---
temp_baseline_file <- paste0(base_dir_baseline, 'ukb.', example_pop, '.sparse.sscore')
if (!file.exists(temp_baseline_file)) {
  stop(paste("Could not find file to get column names:", temp_baseline_file))
}
all_cols <- names(fread(temp_baseline_file, nrows = 0))

# Find the column that matches our trait, method, and is a SUM
param_col <- all_cols[
  str_detect(all_cols, example_trait) & 
  str_detect(all_cols, fixed(example_method, ignore_case = TRUE)) & 
  str_detect(all_cols, 'SUM')
]

if (length(param_col) == 0) {
  stop(paste("Could not find a matching score column for", example_trait, "and", example_method))
}
param_col <- param_col[1]
print(paste("Found matching score column:", param_col))


# --- 3. Loop through props to get data ---
for (prop in props) {
  print(paste("Processing prop:", prop))
  
  # Define file paths
  baseline_file <- paste0(base_dir_baseline, 'ukb.', example_pop, '.sparse.sscore')
  # --- Make sure this path is correct for your 'random' masking ---
  masked_file <- paste0(base_dir_masked, 'ukb.', example_pop, '.sparse.random.prop_', prop, '.sscore')
  
  if (!file.exists(baseline_file) || !file.exists(masked_file)) {
    print(paste("Skipping prop", prop, "- files not found"))
    next
  }
  
  # --- NEW: Get correction factors from your results file ---
  correction_factors <- results_dt[
    trait == example_trait & 
    population == example_pop & 
    prop == .GlobalEnv$prop & 
    param == param_col
  ]
  
  if (nrow(correction_factors) == 0) {
    print(paste("Skipping prop", prop, "- No correction factors found in results_dt"))
    next
  }
  
  # Get the single scalar value for each
  obs_v_rel <- correction_factors$obs_rel_var
  est_v_rel <- correction_factors$est_rel_var
```
