
***

## Random masking

Note. here we are using EUR LD reference data to estimate relative PGS R2 and variance, so will only be accurate for a EUR target sample.

```{r}

setwd('~/GenoPred/pipeline/')
library(data.table)
library(GenoUtils)
library(foreach)
library(doMC)
registerDoMC(8)

source('../functions/misc.R')
source_all('../functions')

pgs_methods <- 'sbayesrc'
populations <- c('AFR', 'AMR', 'CSA', 'EAS', 'EUR', 'MID')
traits <- c('HT', 'HB', 'PLT', 'BMI', 'BWT', 'MCHC', 'SBP', 'NEU', 'HDL', 'TC')

# Read in score file
score_file<-fread('/mnt/project/mind_the_gap/score_file_sparse.txt')

for(i in 1-(c(0.5,0.25,0.1,0.05))){
  rel_r2_all<-NULL
  for(pop in c('EUR','AFR')){
    print(i)
    # Read in list of randomly retained variants
    unmasked <- fread(paste0('~/random_snps.',i,'.txt'), header = F)$V1
    f_miss <- data.table(SNP = score_file$SNP, F_MISS = 1)
    f_miss$F_MISS[f_miss$SNP %in% unmasked] <- 0
    
    # Calculate relative R2 (on each chromosome like in GenoPred)
    rel_r2 <- rel_pgs_r2_missing_eigen(
        ld_dir = '~/ukbEUR_HM3',
        score_df = score_file,
        f_miss = f_miss)
        
    rel_r2$population <- pop
    
    rel_r2_all <- rbind(rel_r2_all, rel_r2)
    
  }
  write.table(
    rel_r2_all, 
    paste0(
      '~/est_rsq.sparse.random.',i,'.txt'
    ), col.names = T, row.names = F, quote = F)
}

```

***

## Imputed genotypes

Lets estimate the relative PGS R2 and variance when using the imputed genotype data. It should be high for the EUR population, but a bit lower for the AFR UKB population.

```{r}

setwd('~/GenoPred/pipeline/')
library(data.table)
library(GenoUtils)
library(foreach)
library(doMC)
registerDoMC(8)

source('../functions/misc.R')
source_all('../functions')

pgs_methods <- 'sbayesrc'
populations <- c('AFR', 'EUR')
traits <- c('HT', 'HB', 'PLT', 'BMI', 'BWT', 'MCHC', 'SBP', 'NEU', 'HDL', 'TC')

# Read in score file
score_file<-fread('/mnt/project/mind_the_gap/score_file_sparse.txt')

for(pop in populations){
  print(pop)
  
  # Read in imputation INFO data
  rsq <- foreach(i = 1:22, .combine = rbind, .options.multicore = list(preschedule = FALSE)) %dopar% {
    tmp <- fread(
      cmd = paste0(
        "grep -v '^#' /mnt/project/mind_the_gap/genotype/ukb_imp_sbayesrc_",
        pop,
        '.chr',
        i,
        '.snp-stats.txt'
      ),
      nThread = 1
    )
  tmp
  }
  
  rsq <- rsq[rsq$rsid %in% score_file$SNP,]
  rsq$info[rsq$info < 0.3] <- 0 # We removed variants with INFO < 0.3
  f_miss <- data.table(SNP = rsq$rsid, F_MISS = 1 - rsq$info)

  # Calculate relative R2 (on each chromosome like in GenoPred)
  rel_r2 <- rel_pgs_r2_missing_eigen(
      ld_dir = paste0('~/ukb', pop,'_HM3'),
      score_df = score_file,
      f_miss = f_miss)
      
  write.table(
    rel_r2, 
    paste0(
      '~/est_rsq.sparse.imputed.',pop,'.txt'
    ), col.names = T, row.names = F, quote = F)
}

```

```{bash}
dx upload est_rsq.sparse.imputed.*.txt --path mind_the_gap/score_files/
```

***

# Compare estimated and observed

## Random masking

```{r}

library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)

# --- 1. Data Processing Loop ---
# This section is an enhanced version of your original code.

# Define analysis parameters
populations <- c('EUR')
traits <- c('HT', 'HB', 'PLT', 'BMI', 'BWT', 'MCHC', 'SBP', 'NEU', 'HDL', 'TC')
base_dir_baseline <- '~/pgs_wgs/sparse/baseline/'
base_dir_masked <- '~/pgs_wgs/sparse/masked_random/'
pheno_dir <- '/mnt/project/mind_the_gap/phenotype/'
call_rate <- 1-(c(0.5,0.25,0.05))

results <- list()

n_boot <- 100 # Number of bootstrap replicates (1000 is good, 100 for testing)
set.seed(123)  # For reproducible results

for (pop in populations) {
    print(paste("Processing Population:", pop))
    # Construct file paths and check for existence
    baseline_file <- paste0(base_dir_baseline, 'ukb.', pop, '.sparse.sscore')
    
    for(call_rate_i in call_rate){
      print(paste("  ... Call rate:", call_rate_i))

      # Read in estimated R2 and variance
      est <- fread(paste0('~/est_rsq.sparse.random.', call_rate_i, '.txt'))
      
      masked_file <- paste0(base_dir_masked, 'ukb.', pop, '.', call_rate_i, '.sparse.sscore')
      
      if (!file.exists(baseline_file) || !file.exists(masked_file)) next
      
      pgs_baseline <- fread(baseline_file)
      pgs_masked <- fread(masked_file)
  
      for (trait in traits) {
          print(paste("  ... Trait:", trait))
          pheno_file <- paste0(pheno_dir, trait, '.', pop, '.txt')
          if (!file.exists(pheno_file)) next
          
          pheno <- fread(pheno_file)
  
          # Subset PGS data
          pgs_baseline_i <- pgs_baseline[, c('IID', names(pgs_baseline)[grepl('SUM', names(pgs_baseline)) &
                                                                      grepl(trait, names(pgs_baseline))]), with = F]
          pgs_masked_i <- pgs_masked[, c('IID', names(pgs_masked)[grepl('SUM', names(pgs_masked)) &
                                                                  grepl(trait, names(pgs_masked))]), with = F]
          
          # Merge PGS data
          pgs_baseline_pheno <- merge(pheno, pgs_baseline_i, by = 'IID', sort = FALSE)
          pgs_masked_pheno <- merge(pheno, pgs_masked_i, by = 'IID', sort = FALSE)
          
          param_cols <- names(pgs_baseline_i)[-1]
  
          for (param in param_cols) {
              if (!(param %in% names(pgs_masked_pheno))) next
              
              n <- nrow(pgs_baseline_pheno)
              
              # --- 1.5. BOOTSTRAPPING SECTION ---
              boot_stats <- replicate(n_boot, {
                  indices <- sample(1:n, n, replace = TRUE)
                  
                  boot_base <- pgs_baseline_pheno[indices]
                  boot_masked <- pgs_masked_pheno[indices]
                  
                  pheno_vals_base <- boot_base[[trait]]
                  pgs_vals_base <- boot_base[[param]]
                  
                  pheno_vals_masked <- boot_masked[[trait]]
                  pgs_vals_masked <- boot_masked[[param]]
                  
                  # R2 stats
                  r2_base_boot <- tryCatch(cor(pheno_vals_base, pgs_vals_base)^2, error = function(e) NA)
                  r2_masked_boot <- tryCatch(cor(pheno_vals_masked, pgs_vals_masked)^2, error = function(e) NA)
                  
                  # *** NEW: Calculate Relative Variance ***
                  var_base_boot <- tryCatch(var(pgs_vals_base), error = function(e) NA)
                  var_masked_boot <- tryCatch(var(pgs_vals_masked), error = function(e) NA)
                  rel_var_boot <- if (is.na(var_base_boot) || is.na(var_masked_boot) || var_base_boot == 0) {
                      NA
                  } else {
                      var_masked_boot / var_base_boot
                  }
                  
                  # Correlation stat
                  cor_base_masked_boot <- tryCatch(cor(pgs_vals_base, pgs_vals_masked), error = function(e) NA)
                  cor_base_masked_sq_boot <- cor_base_masked_boot^2
                  
                  # Relative R2
                  rel_r2_boot <- if (is.na(r2_base_boot) || is.na(r2_masked_boot) || r2_base_boot == 0) {
                      NA
                  } else {
                      r2_masked_boot / r2_base_boot
                  }
                  
                  # Return a named vector
                  c(
                      r2_base = r2_base_boot,
                      r2_masked = r2_masked_boot,
                      rel_var = rel_var_boot, # *** NEW ***
                      cor_base_masked = cor_base_masked_boot,
                      cor_base_masked_sq = cor_base_masked_sq_boot,
                      rel_r2 = rel_r2_boot
                  )
              })
              
              # Transpose boot_stats
              boot_stats_t <- t(boot_stats)
              
              # --- Calculate 95% CIs (using the percentile method) ---
              safe_quantile <- function(col_name) {
                  quantile(boot_stats_t[, col_name], c(0.025, 0.975), na.rm = TRUE)
              }
              
              r2_base_ci <- safe_quantile("r2_base")
              r2_masked_ci <- safe_quantile("r2_masked")
              rel_var_ci <- safe_quantile("rel_var") # *** NEW ***
              cor_base_masked_ci <- safe_quantile("cor_base_masked")
              cor_base_masked_sq_ci <- safe_quantile("cor_base_masked_sq")
              rel_r2_ci <- safe_quantile("rel_r2")
              # --- END BOOTSTRAPPING ---
  
              
              # --- 1.6. Calculate Point Estimates ---
              r2_baseline_pheno <- cor(pgs_baseline_pheno[[trait]], pgs_baseline_pheno[[param]])^2
              r2_masked_pheno <- cor(pgs_masked_pheno[[trait]], pgs_masked_pheno[[param]])^2
              p_val <- coef(summary(lm(pgs_baseline_pheno[[trait]] ~ pgs_baseline_pheno[[param]])))[2,4]
              cor_base_masked <- cor(pgs_baseline_pheno[[param]], pgs_masked_pheno[[param]])
              cor_baseline_masked_sq <- cor_base_masked^2
              
              # *** NEW: Point estimate for relative variance ***
              var_baseline <- var(pgs_baseline_pheno[[param]])
              var_masked <- var(pgs_masked_pheno[[param]])
              rel_var_point <- var_masked / var_baseline
  
              # Your 'est' object logic
              est_i <- est[est$beta_set == gsub('_SUM','',param),]
              
              # --- 1.7. Store results (expanded data.table) ---
              results[[length(results) + 1]] <- data.table(
                  param = param,
                  trait = trait,
                  population = pop,
                  call_rate = call_rate_i,
                  
                  # Point estimates
                  r2_baseline_pheno = r2_baseline_pheno,
                  r2_masked_pheno = r2_masked_pheno,
                  cor_baseline_masked = cor_base_masked,
                  cor_baseline_masked_sq = cor_baseline_masked_sq,
                  obs_rel_r2 = r2_masked_pheno / r2_baseline_pheno,
                  obs_rel_var = rel_var_point, # *** NEW ***
                  p_baseline_pheno = p_val,
                  n = nrow(pgs_baseline_pheno),
  
                  # From user's 'est' object
                  est_rel_var = est_i$relative_variance,
                  est_rel_r2 = est_i$relative_R2,
                  
                  # --- 95% CIs ---
                  
                  # R2 Baseline CIs
                  r2_baseline_low_ci = r2_base_ci[1],
                  r2_baseline_high_ci = r2_base_ci[2],
                  
                  # R2 Masked CIs
                  r2_masked_low_ci = r2_masked_ci[1],
                  r2_masked_high_ci = r2_masked_ci[2],
                  
                  # *** NEW: Relative Variance CIs ***
                  rel_var_low_ci = rel_var_ci[1],
                  rel_var_high_ci = rel_var_ci[2],
                  
                  # Cor Baseline-Masked CIs
                  cor_baseline_masked_low_ci = cor_base_masked_ci[1],
                  cor_baseline_masked_high_ci = cor_base_masked_ci[2],
                  
                  # Squared Cor Baseline-Masked CIs
                  cor_baseline_masked_sq_low_ci = cor_base_masked_sq_ci[1],
                  cor_baseline_masked_sq_high_ci = cor_base_masked_sq_ci[2],
                          
                  # Relative R2 CIs
                  rel_r2_low_ci = rel_r2_ci[1],
                  rel_r2_high_ci = rel_r2_ci[2]
              )
          }
      }
    }
}

results_dt <- rbindlist(results)
results_dt[, pgs_method := ifelse(str_detect(param, 'sbayesrc'), 'SBayesRC', 'pT+clump')]
results_dt$call_rate<-paste0('Call rate = ', results_dt$call_rate)

# Compare squared correlation between PGS and observed relative R2
ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC', ], 
       aes(x = cor_baseline_masked_sq, y = obs_rel_r2, colour = trait)) +
  geom_abline(colour = 'red') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = cor_baseline_masked_sq_low_ci, 
                    xmax = cor_baseline_masked_sq_high_ci), width=0) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_r2_low_ci, 
                    ymax = rel_r2_high_ci), width=0) +
  facet_grid(. ~ call_rate) +
#  coord_fixed() +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = "Squared correlation between PGS (95%CI)", 
       y = "Observed relative R2 (95%CI)",
       colour = NULL)

# Compare squared correlation between PGS and estimated relative R2
ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC', ], 
       aes(x = est_rel_r2, y = cor_baseline_masked_sq, colour = trait)) +
  geom_abline(colour = 'red') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = cor_baseline_masked_sq_low_ci, 
                    ymax = cor_baseline_masked_sq_high_ci)) +
  facet_grid(. ~ call_rate) +
#  coord_fixed() +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = 'Estimated relative R2', 
       y = "Squared correlation\nbetween PGS (95%CI)",
       colour = NULL)

# Compare observed relative R2 and estimated relative R2
ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC', ], 
       aes(x = est_rel_r2, y = obs_rel_r2, colour = trait)) +
  geom_abline(colour = 'red') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_r2_low_ci, 
                    ymax = rel_r2_high_ci)) +
  facet_grid(. ~ call_rate) +
#  coord_fixed() +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = 'Estimated relative R2', 
       y = 'Observed relative R2',
       colour = NULL)

# Compare observed relative variance and estimated relative variance
ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC', ], 
       aes(x = est_rel_var, y = obs_rel_var, colour = trait)) +
  geom_abline(colour = 'red') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_var_low_ci, 
                    ymax = rel_var_high_ci), width = 0) +
  facet_grid(. ~ call_rate) +
#  coord_fixed() +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = 'Estimated relative variance', 
       y = "Observed relative variance (95%CI)",
       colour = NULL)

# This is good. It shows that our estimator of relative R2 and variance is highly accurate in real data.

```

***

## Imputed genotypes

```{bash}
dx download mind_the_gap/pgs.tar
tar -xvf pgs.tar

dx download mind_the_gap/pgs_wgs.tar
tar -xvf pgs_wgs.tar
```

```{r}

library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(cowplot)
library(stringr)

# --- 1. Data Processing Loop ---
# This section is an enhanced version of your original code.

# Define analysis parameters
populations <- c('AFR', 'EUR')
traits <- c('HT', 'HB', 'PLT', 'BMI', 'BWT', 'MCHC', 'SBP', 'NEU', 'HDL', 'TC')
base_dir_baseline <- '~/pgs_wgs/sparse/baseline/'
base_dir_masked <- '~/pgs/sparse/baseline/'
pheno_dir <- '/mnt/project/mind_the_gap/phenotype/'
mask_thresh <- c(0.3)

results <- list()

n_boot <- 100 # Number of bootstrap replicates (1000 is good, 100 for testing)
set.seed(123)  # For reproducible results

for (pop in populations) {
    print(paste("Processing Population:", pop))
    # Construct file paths and check for existence
    baseline_file <- paste0(base_dir_baseline, 'ukb.', pop, '.sparse.sscore')
    
    for(mask_thresh_i in mask_thresh){
      print(paste("  ... Mask thresh:", mask_thresh_i))

      # Read in estimated R2 and variance
      est <- fread(paste0('~/est_rsq.sparse.imputed.',pop,'.txt'))
      
      masked_file <- paste0(base_dir_masked, 'ukb.', pop, '.sparse.sscore')
      
      if (!file.exists(baseline_file) || !file.exists(masked_file)) next
      
      pgs_baseline <- fread(baseline_file)
      pgs_masked <- fread(masked_file)
  
      pgs_baseline <- pgs_baseline[pgs_baseline$IID %in% pgs_masked$IID, ]
      pgs_masked <- pgs_masked[pgs_masked$IID %in% pgs_baseline$IID, ]

      for (trait in traits) {
          print(paste("  ... Trait:", trait))
          pheno_file <- paste0(pheno_dir, trait, '.', pop, '.txt')
          if (!file.exists(pheno_file)) next
          
          pheno <- fread(pheno_file)
  
          # Subset PGS data
          pgs_baseline_i <- pgs_baseline[, c('IID', names(pgs_baseline)[grepl('SUM', names(pgs_baseline)) &
                                                                      grepl(trait, names(pgs_baseline))]), with = F]
          pgs_masked_i <- pgs_masked[, c('IID', names(pgs_masked)[grepl('SUM', names(pgs_masked)) &
                                                                  grepl(trait, names(pgs_masked))]), with = F]
          
          # Merge PGS data
          pgs_baseline_pheno <- merge(pheno, pgs_baseline_i, by = 'IID', sort = FALSE)
          pgs_masked_pheno <- merge(pheno, pgs_masked_i, by = 'IID', sort = FALSE)
          
          param_cols <- names(pgs_baseline_i)[-1]
  
          for (param in param_cols) {
              if (!(param %in% names(pgs_masked_pheno))) next
              
              n <- nrow(pgs_baseline_pheno)
              
              # --- 1.5. BOOTSTRAPPING SECTION ---
              boot_stats <- replicate(n_boot, {
                  indices <- sample(1:n, n, replace = TRUE)
                  
                  boot_base <- pgs_baseline_pheno[indices]
                  boot_masked <- pgs_masked_pheno[indices]
                  
                  pheno_vals_base <- boot_base[[trait]]
                  pgs_vals_base <- boot_base[[param]]
                  
                  pheno_vals_masked <- boot_masked[[trait]]
                  pgs_vals_masked <- boot_masked[[param]]
                  
                  # R2 stats
                  r2_base_boot <- tryCatch(cor(pheno_vals_base, pgs_vals_base)^2, error = function(e) NA)
                  r2_masked_boot <- tryCatch(cor(pheno_vals_masked, pgs_vals_masked)^2, error = function(e) NA)
                  
                  # *** NEW: Calculate Relative Variance ***
                  var_base_boot <- tryCatch(var(pgs_vals_base), error = function(e) NA)
                  var_masked_boot <- tryCatch(var(pgs_vals_masked), error = function(e) NA)
                  rel_var_boot <- if (is.na(var_base_boot) || is.na(var_masked_boot) || var_base_boot == 0) {
                      NA
                  } else {
                      var_masked_boot / var_base_boot
                  }
                  
                  # Correlation stat
                  cor_base_masked_boot <- tryCatch(cor(pgs_vals_base, pgs_vals_masked), error = function(e) NA)
                  cor_base_masked_sq_boot <- cor_base_masked_boot^2
                  
                  # Relative R2
                  rel_r2_boot <- if (is.na(r2_base_boot) || is.na(r2_masked_boot) || r2_base_boot == 0) {
                      NA
                  } else {
                      r2_masked_boot / r2_base_boot
                  }
                  
                  # Return a named vector
                  c(
                      r2_base = r2_base_boot,
                      r2_masked = r2_masked_boot,
                      rel_var = rel_var_boot, # *** NEW ***
                      cor_base_masked = cor_base_masked_boot,
                      cor_base_masked_sq = cor_base_masked_sq_boot,
                      rel_r2 = rel_r2_boot
                  )
              })
              
              # Transpose boot_stats
              boot_stats_t <- t(boot_stats)
              
              # --- Calculate 95% CIs (using the percentile method) ---
              safe_quantile <- function(col_name) {
                  quantile(boot_stats_t[, col_name], c(0.025, 0.975), na.rm = TRUE)
              }
              
              r2_base_ci <- safe_quantile("r2_base")
              r2_masked_ci <- safe_quantile("r2_masked")
              rel_var_ci <- safe_quantile("rel_var") # *** NEW ***
              cor_base_masked_ci <- safe_quantile("cor_base_masked")
              cor_base_masked_sq_ci <- safe_quantile("cor_base_masked_sq")
              rel_r2_ci <- safe_quantile("rel_r2")
              # --- END BOOTSTRAPPING ---
  
              
              # --- 1.6. Calculate Point Estimates ---
              r2_baseline_pheno <- cor(pgs_baseline_pheno[[trait]], pgs_baseline_pheno[[param]])^2
              r2_masked_pheno <- cor(pgs_masked_pheno[[trait]], pgs_masked_pheno[[param]])^2
              p_val <- coef(summary(lm(pgs_baseline_pheno[[trait]] ~ pgs_baseline_pheno[[param]])))[2,4]
              cor_base_masked <- cor(pgs_baseline_pheno[[param]], pgs_masked_pheno[[param]])
              cor_baseline_masked_sq <- cor_base_masked^2
              
              # *** NEW: Point estimate for relative variance ***
              var_baseline <- var(pgs_baseline_pheno[[param]])
              var_masked <- var(pgs_masked_pheno[[param]])
              rel_var_point <- var_masked / var_baseline
  
              # Your 'est' object logic
              est_i <- est[est$beta_set == gsub('_SUM','',param),]
              
              # --- 1.7. Store results (expanded data.table) ---
              results[[length(results) + 1]] <- data.table(
                  param = param,
                  trait = trait,
                  population = pop,
                  mask_thresh = mask_thresh_i,
                  
                  # Point estimates
                  r2_baseline_pheno = r2_baseline_pheno,
                  r2_masked_pheno = r2_masked_pheno,
                  cor_baseline_masked = cor_base_masked,
                  cor_baseline_masked_sq = cor_baseline_masked_sq,
                  obs_rel_r2 = r2_masked_pheno / r2_baseline_pheno,
                  obs_rel_var = rel_var_point, # *** NEW ***
                  p_baseline_pheno = p_val,
                  n = nrow(pgs_baseline_pheno),
  
                  # From user's 'est' object
                  est_rel_var = est_i$relative_variance,
                  est_rel_r2 = est_i$relative_R2,
                  
                  # --- 95% CIs ---
                  
                  # R2 Baseline CIs
                  r2_baseline_low_ci = r2_base_ci[1],
                  r2_baseline_high_ci = r2_base_ci[2],
                  
                  # R2 Masked CIs
                  r2_masked_low_ci = r2_masked_ci[1],
                  r2_masked_high_ci = r2_masked_ci[2],
                  
                  # *** NEW: Relative Variance CIs ***
                  rel_var_low_ci = rel_var_ci[1],
                  rel_var_high_ci = rel_var_ci[2],
                  
                  # Cor Baseline-Masked CIs
                  cor_baseline_masked_low_ci = cor_base_masked_ci[1],
                  cor_baseline_masked_high_ci = cor_base_masked_ci[2],
                  
                  # Squared Cor Baseline-Masked CIs
                  cor_baseline_masked_sq_low_ci = cor_base_masked_sq_ci[1],
                  cor_baseline_masked_sq_high_ci = cor_base_masked_sq_ci[2],
                          
                  # Relative R2 CIs
                  rel_r2_low_ci = rel_r2_ci[1],
                  rel_r2_high_ci = rel_r2_ci[2]
              )
          }
      }
    }
}

results_dt <- rbindlist(results)
results_dt[, pgs_method := ifelse(str_detect(param, 'sbayesrc'), 'SBayesRC', 'pT+clump')]
results_dt$call_rate<-paste0('Call rate = ', results_dt$call_rate)

# Plot the relative R2 between WGS and imputed genotype
ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC' & r2_baseline_pheno > 0.01, ], 
       aes(x = trait, y = obs_rel_r2, colour = trait)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_r2_low_ci, 
                    ymax = rel_r2_high_ci)) +
  facet_wrap(. ~ population, scales = 'free') +
#  coord_fixed() +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = "Trait", 
       y = "Observed relative R2 (95%CI)",
       colour = NULL)
# There is no significant difference between the R2 of PGS from genotype or WGS data

# Plot the relative variance between WGS and imputed genotype
ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC' & r2_baseline_pheno > 0.01, ], 
       aes(x = trait, y = obs_rel_var, colour = trait)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_var_low_ci, 
                    ymax = rel_var_high_ci)) +
  facet_wrap(. ~ population, scales = 'free') +
#  coord_fixed() +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = "Trait", 
       y = "Observed relative variance (95%CI)",
       colour = NULL)
# Genotype-based PGS have slightly lower variance than WGS PGS

# Plot the squared correlation between WGS and imputed genotype
ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC' & r2_baseline_pheno > 0.01, ], 
       aes(x = trait, y = cor_baseline_masked_sq, colour = trait)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = cor_baseline_masked_sq_low_ci, 
                    ymax = cor_baseline_masked_sq_high_ci)) +
  facet_wrap(. ~ population, scales = 'free') +
#  coord_fixed() +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = "Trait", 
       y = "Squared correlation between PGS (95%CI)",
       colour = NULL)
# There is no significant difference between the R2 of PGS from genotype or WGS data

# Compare squared correlation between PGS and observed relative R2
ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC' & r2_baseline_pheno > 0.01, ], 
       aes(x = cor_baseline_masked_sq, y = obs_rel_r2, colour = trait)) +
  geom_abline(colour = 'red') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin = cor_baseline_masked_sq_low_ci, 
                    xmax = cor_baseline_masked_sq_high_ci)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_r2_low_ci, 
                    ymax = rel_r2_high_ci)) +
  facet_wrap(. ~ population, scales = 'free') +
#  coord_fixed() +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = "Squared correlation between PGS (95%CI)", 
       y = "Observed relative R2 (95%CI)",
       colour = NULL)

# Compare squared correlation between PGS and estimated relative R2
ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC' & r2_baseline_pheno > 0.01, ], 
       aes(x = est_rel_r2, y = cor_baseline_masked_sq, colour = trait)) +
  geom_abline(colour = 'red') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = cor_baseline_masked_sq_low_ci, 
                    ymax = cor_baseline_masked_sq_high_ci)) +
  facet_wrap(. ~ population, scales = 'free') +
#  coord_fixed() +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = 'Estimated relative R2', 
       y = "Squared correlation\nbetween PGS (95%CI)",
       colour = NULL)
# Note. squared correlation between PGS is lower than estimated and observed relative R2.

# Compare observed relative R2 and estimated relative R2
ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC' & r2_baseline_pheno > 0.01, ], 
       aes(x = est_rel_r2, y = obs_rel_r2, colour = trait)) +
  geom_abline(colour = 'red') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_r2_low_ci, 
                    ymax = rel_r2_high_ci)) +
  facet_wrap(. ~ population, scales = 'free') +
#  coord_fixed() +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = 'Estimated relative R2', 
       y = 'Observed relative R2',
       colour = NULL)

# Compare observed relative variance and estimated relative variance
ggplot(results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC' & r2_baseline_pheno > 0.01, ], 
       aes(x = est_rel_var, y = obs_rel_var, colour = trait)) +
  geom_abline(colour = 'red') +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_var_low_ci, 
                    ymax = rel_var_high_ci), width = 0) +
  facet_wrap(. ~ population, scales = 'free') +
#  coord_fixed() +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(x = 'Estimated relative variance', 
       y = "Observed relative variance (95%CI)",
       colour = NULL)

# Estimated relative variance is slightly lower than observed., particularly in AFR population.

# This is a bit odd, as correlation between PGS is lower than expected but relative variance is higher.
```

***

# OLD
## Compare baseline and masked PGS

We want to see whether the estimated relative PGS R2 and variance is similar to the observed. 

```{bash}
dx download mind_the_gap/pgs_wgs.tar
tar -cvf pgs_wgs.tar pgs_wgs/
```

```{r}

library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)

# --- 1. Data Processing Loop ---
# This section is an enhanced version of your original code.

# Define analysis parameters
populations <- c('AFR', 'AMR', 'CSA', 'EAS', 'EUR', 'MID')
traits <- c('HT', 'HB', 'PLT', 'BMI', 'BWT', 'MCHC', 'SBP', 'NEU', 'HDL', 'TC')
base_dir_baseline <- '~/pgs_wgs/sparse/baseline/'
base_dir_masked <- '~/pgs_wgs/sparse/masked/'
pheno_dir <- '/mnt/project/mind_the_gap/phenotype/'

results <- list()
for (pop in populations) {
    print(pop)
    # Construct file paths and check for existence
    baseline_file <- paste0(base_dir_baseline, 'ukb.', pop, '.sparse.sscore')
    masked_file <- paste0(base_dir_masked, 'ukb.', pop, '.sparse.sscore')
    if (!file.exists(baseline_file) || !file.exists(masked_file)) next
    
    pgs_baseline <- fread(baseline_file)
    pgs_masked <- fread(masked_file)

    for (trait in traits) {
        print(trait)
        pheno_file <- paste0(pheno_dir, trait, '.', pop, '.txt')
        if (!file.exists(pheno_file)) next
        
        pheno <- fread(pheno_file)

        # Subset PGS data
        pgs_baseline_i <- pgs_baseline[, c('IID', names(pgs_baseline)[grepl('SUM', names(pgs_baseline)) &
                                                                        grepl(trait, names(pgs_baseline))]), with = F]
        pgs_masked_i <- pgs_masked[, c('IID', names(pgs_masked)[grepl('SUM', names(pgs_masked)) &
                                                                  grepl(trait, names(pgs_masked))]), with = F]
        
        # Merge PGS data
        pgs_baseline_pheno <- merge(pheno, pgs_baseline_i, by = 'IID', sort = FALSE)
        pgs_masked_pheno <- merge(pheno, pgs_masked_i, by = 'IID', sort = FALSE)
        
        param_cols <- names(pgs_baseline_i)[-1]

        for (param in param_cols) {
            if (!(param %in% names(pgs_masked_pheno))) next
            
            r2_baseline_pheno <- cor(pgs_baseline_pheno[[trait]], pgs_baseline_pheno[[param]])^2
            r2_masked_pheno <- cor(pgs_masked_pheno[[trait]], pgs_masked_pheno[[param]])^2
            
            # Get p-value for the PGS term in the baseline model
            p_val <- coef(summary(lm(pgs_baseline_pheno[[trait]] ~ pgs_baseline_pheno[[param]])))[2,4]
            
            # Calculate correlation between scores for the estimated metric
            cor_base_masked <- cor(pgs_baseline_pheno[[param]], pgs_masked_pheno[[param]])

            results[[length(results) + 1]] <- data.table(
                param = param,
                trait = trait,
                population = pop,
                r2_baseline_pheno = r2_baseline_pheno,
                r2_masked_pheno = r2_masked_pheno,
                cor_baseline_masked = cor_base_masked,
                p_baseline_pheno = p_val,
                n = nrow(pgs_baseline_pheno)
            )
        }
    }
}

results_dt <- rbindlist(results)

# Identify the PGS method for plotting
results_dt[, pgs_method := ifelse(str_detect(param, 'sbayesrc'), 'SBayesRC', 'pT+clump')]

# --- 2. Calculate Final Metrics and Filter ---

# Calculate the observed and estimated relative R2 from the new metrics
results_dt[, obs_rel_r2 := r2_masked_pheno / r2_baseline_pheno]
results_dt[, est_rel_r2 := cor_baseline_masked^2]


library(boot)
library(dplyr)

# Function to bootstrap mean and CI
bootstrap_ci <- function(x, n_boot = 1000) {
  boot_res <- boot(x, function(data, i) mean(data[i], na.rm = TRUE), R = n_boot)
  ci <- boot.ci(boot_res, type = "perc")$percent[4:5]
  list(mean = mean(x, na.rm = TRUE), lower = ci[1], upper = ci[2])
}

# Apply bootstrapping by population and method
agg_results <- results_dt[, {
  r2_base_ci <- bootstrap_ci(r2_baseline_pheno)
  r2_masked_ci <- bootstrap_ci(r2_masked_pheno)
  rel_ci <- bootstrap_ci(obs_rel_r2)
  
  data.table(
    r2_baseline_mean = r2_base_ci$mean,
    r2_baseline_lower = r2_base_ci$lower,
    r2_baseline_upper = r2_base_ci$upper,
    r2_masked_mean = r2_masked_ci$mean,
    r2_masked_lower = r2_masked_ci$lower,
    r2_masked_upper = r2_masked_ci$upper,
    rel_mean = rel_ci$mean,
    rel_lower = rel_ci$lower,
    rel_upper = rel_ci$upper
  )
}, by = .(population, pgs_method)]


# --- Enhanced Feature: More robust filtering ---
# Filter based on both statistical significance (p < 0.05) AND a more stringent
# minimum effect size (baseline partial R² > 0.5%) to remove noisy, weakly predictive scores.
results_clean <- results_dt[p_baseline_pheno < 0.001 & r2_baseline_pheno > 0.02]

# Remove MID and AMR populations as the same size is small, and admixture complicates interpretation
# results_clean <- results_clean[results_clean$population %in% c('AFR','CSA','EAS','EUR'),]

# --- 3. Generate Visualizations ---

# Plot 1: The "Money Plot" - Estimated vs. Observed Relative R²
# This plot is the core validation of your analytical estimator.
validation_plot <- ggplot(results_clean, aes(x = est_rel_r2, y = obs_rel_r2)) +
    geom_point(aes(color = population), alpha = 0.8, size = 2.5) +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed", size = 1) +
    facet_wrap(~pgs_method) +
    scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
    coord_cartesian(
      xlim = c(min(results_clean$est_rel_r2, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.98, NA),
      ylim = c(min(results_clean$obs_rel_r2, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.98, NA) # Zoom in but allow some points > 100%
    ) +
    labs(
        title = "Validation: Estimated vs. Observed Relative R²",
        subtitle = "Filtered for baseline PGS with p < 0.001 and partial R² > 2%",
        x = "Estimated Relative R² (cor(PGS_baseline, PGS_masked)²)",
        y = "Observed Relative Phenotypic R² ((R²_masked) / (R²_baseline))",
        color = "Population"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 14)

print(validation_plot)

# Plot 2: Portability Across Ancestries
# This plot clearly shows how performance drops in different populations.
portability_plot <- ggplot(results_clean, aes(x = population, y = obs_rel_r2, fill = population)) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.9) +
    facet_wrap(~pgs_method) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
    labs(
        title = "Observed PGS Portability Across Ancestries (Adjusted for Covariates)",
        subtitle = "Performance drop when masking variants with INFO < 0.95 (filtered for strong PGS)",
        x = "Population",
        y = "Observed Relative Phenotypic R²"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), legend.position = "none")

print(portability_plot)

```

This shows that there is not 

***

## Compare baseline and randomly masked PGS

```{r}

library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)

# --- 1. Data Processing Loop ---
# This section is an enhanced version of your original code.

# Define analysis parameters
populations <- c('AFR', 'AMR', 'CSA', 'EAS', 'EUR', 'MID')
traits <- c('HT', 'HB', 'PLT', 'BMI', 'BWT', 'MCHC', 'SBP', 'NEU', 'HDL', 'TC')
base_dir_baseline <- '~/pgs_wgs/sparse/baseline/'
base_dir_masked <- '~/pgs_wgs/sparse/masked_random/'
pheno_dir <- '/mnt/project/mind_the_gap/phenotype/'

results <- list()

for (pop in populations) {
    print(pop)
    # Construct file paths and check for existence
    baseline_file <- paste0(base_dir_baseline, 'ukb.', pop, '.sparse.sscore')
    masked_file <- paste0(base_dir_masked, 'ukb.', pop, '.sparse.sscore')
    if (!file.exists(baseline_file) || !file.exists(masked_file)) next
    
    pgs_baseline <- fread(baseline_file)
    pgs_masked <- fread(masked_file)

    for (trait in traits) {
        print(trait)
        pheno_file <- paste0(pheno_dir, trait, '.', pop, '.txt')
        if (!file.exists(pheno_file)) next
        
        pheno <- fread(pheno_file)

        # Subset PGS data
        pgs_baseline_i <- pgs_baseline[, c('IID', names(pgs_baseline)[grepl('SUM', names(pgs_baseline)) &
                                                                        grepl(trait, names(pgs_baseline))]), with = F]
        pgs_masked_i <- pgs_masked[, c('IID', names(pgs_masked)[grepl('SUM', names(pgs_masked)) &
                                                                  grepl(trait, names(pgs_masked))]), with = F]
        
        # Merge PGS data
        pgs_baseline_pheno <- merge(pheno, pgs_baseline_i, by = 'IID', sort = FALSE)
        pgs_masked_pheno <- merge(pheno, pgs_masked_i, by = 'IID', sort = FALSE)
        
        param_cols <- names(pgs_baseline_i)[-1]

        for (param in param_cols) {
            if (!(param %in% names(pgs_masked_pheno))) next
            
            r2_baseline_pheno <- cor(pgs_baseline_pheno[[trait]], pgs_baseline_pheno[[param]])^2
            r2_masked_pheno <- cor(pgs_masked_pheno[[trait]], pgs_masked_pheno[[param]])^2
            
            # Get p-value for the PGS term in the baseline model
            p_val <- coef(summary(lm(pgs_baseline_pheno[[trait]] ~ pgs_baseline_pheno[[param]])))[2,4]
            
            # Calculate correlation between scores for the estimated metric
            cor_base_masked <- cor(pgs_baseline_pheno[[param]], pgs_masked_pheno[[param]])

            results[[length(results) + 1]] <- data.table(
                param = param,
                trait = trait,
                population = pop,
                r2_baseline_pheno = r2_baseline_pheno,
                r2_masked_pheno = r2_masked_pheno,
                cor_baseline_masked = cor_base_masked,
                p_baseline_pheno = p_val,
                n = nrow(pgs_baseline_pheno)
            )
        }
    }
}

results_dt <- rbindlist(results)

# Identify the PGS method for plotting
results_dt[, pgs_method := ifelse(str_detect(param, 'sbayesrc'), 'SBayesRC', 'pT+clump')]

# --- 2. Calculate Final Metrics and Filter ---

# Calculate the observed and estimated relative R2 from the new metrics
results_dt[, obs_rel_r2 := r2_masked_pheno / r2_baseline_pheno]
results_dt[, est_rel_r2 := cor_baseline_masked^2]

# --- Enhanced Feature: More robust filtering ---
# Filter based on both statistical significance (p < 0.05) AND a more stringent
# minimum effect size (baseline partial R² > 0.5%) to remove noisy, weakly predictive scores.
results_clean <- results_dt[p_baseline_pheno < 0.001 & r2_baseline_pheno > 0.02]

# Remove MID and AMR populations as the same size is small, and admixture complicates interpretation
#results_clean <- results_clean[results_clean$population %in% c('AFR','CSA','EAS','EUR'),]

# Remove ptclump results except pt1e-4. This is simplify plots
results_clean <- results_clean[results_clean$pgs_method == 'SBayesRC' |
                                 (results_clean$pgs_method == 'pT+clump' &
                                    grepl('1e-04', results_clean$param)), ]

# --- 3. Generate Visualizations ---

# Plot 1: The "Money Plot" - Estimated vs. Observed Relative R²
# This plot is the core validation of your analytical estimator.
validation_plot <- ggplot(results_clean, aes(x = est_rel_r2, y = obs_rel_r2)) +
    geom_point(aes(color = population), alpha = 0.8, size = 2.5) +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed", size = 1) +
    facet_wrap(~pgs_method) +
    scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
    coord_cartesian(
      xlim = c(min(results_clean$est_rel_r2, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.98, NA),
      ylim = c(min(results_clean$obs_rel_r2, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.98, NA) # Zoom in but allow some points > 100%
    ) +
    labs(
        title = "Validation: Estimated vs. Observed Relative R²",
        subtitle = "Filtered for baseline PGS with p < 0.001 and partial R² > 2%",
        x = "Estimated Relative R² (cor(PGS_baseline, PGS_masked)²)",
        y = "Observed Relative Phenotypic R² ((R²_masked) / (R²_baseline))",
        color = "Population"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 14)

print(validation_plot)

# Calculate the mean and standard error for each group
results_summary <- results_clean[, .(
    mean_obs_rel_r2 = mean(obs_rel_r2, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_obs_rel_r2 = sd(obs_rel_r2, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(.N),
    mean_est_rel_r2 = mean(est_rel_r2, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_est_rel_r2 = sd(est_rel_r2, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(.N)
), by = .(pgs_method, population)]


# --- 4. Generate Visualizations ---

# Plot 1: Aggregated "Money Plot" with Error Bars
validation_plot_agg <- ggplot(results_summary, aes(x = mean_est_rel_r2, y = mean_obs_rel_r2, color = population)) +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed", size = 1) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_obs_rel_r2 - se_obs_rel_r2, ymax = mean_obs_rel_r2 + se_obs_rel_r2), width = 0.01) +
    geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = mean_est_rel_r2 - se_est_rel_r2, xmax = mean_est_rel_r2 + se_est_rel_r2), height = 0.01) +
    geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.8) +
    facet_wrap(~pgs_method) +
    scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
    labs(
        title = "Aggregated Validation: Estimated vs. Observed Relative R²",
        subtitle = "Points show mean across traits, error bars show standard error.",
        x = "Estimated Relative R² (cor(PGS_baseline, PGS_masked)²)",
        y = "Observed Relative Phenotypic R² ((Partial R²_masked) / (Partial R²_baseline))",
        color = "Population"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 14)

print(validation_plot_agg)

# Plot 2: Portability Across Ancestries
# This plot clearly shows how performance drops in different populations.
portability_plot <- ggplot(results_clean, aes(x = population, y = obs_rel_r2, fill = population)) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.9) +
    facet_wrap(~pgs_method) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
    labs(
        title = "Observed PGS Portability Across Ancestries (Adjusted for Covariates)",
        subtitle = "Performance drop when masking variants with INFO < 0.95 (filtered for strong PGS)",
        x = "Population",
        y = "Observed Relative Phenotypic R²"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), legend.position = "none")

print(portability_plot)

```

This shows that when missingness is random, the cor(pgs_baseline, pgs_masked)^2 is == to the relative R2 of the PGS.

***

## Compare PGS from WGS and imputed array

```{bash}
dx download mind_the_gap/pgs.tar
tar -xvf pgs.tar
```

```{r}

library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)

# --- 1. Data Processing Loop ---
# This section is an enhanced version of your original code.

# Define analysis parameters
populations <- c('AFR', 'AMR', 'CSA', 'EAS', 'EUR', 'MID')
traits <- c('HT', 'HB', 'PLT', 'BMI', 'BWT', 'MCHC', 'SBP', 'NEU', 'HDL', 'TC')
base_dir_baseline <- '~/pgs_wgs/sparse/baseline/'
base_dir_masked <- '~/pgs/sparse/baseline/'
pheno_dir <- '/mnt/project/mind_the_gap/phenotype/'

# Calculate correlation between all phenotypes in each target population
cors <- list()
for(pop_i in populations){
  pheno_pop_i <- list()
  for(pheno_i in traits){
    pheno_file <- paste0(pheno_dir, pheno_i, '.', pop_i, '.txt')
    pheno_pop_i[[pheno_i]] <- fread(pheno_file)
    names(pheno_pop_i[[pheno_i]])[3] <- pheno_i
  }
  
  pheno_pop_i_merged <- merged_df <- Reduce(function(x, y) merge(x, y, all = TRUE, by = c('FID','IID')), pheno_pop_i)

  cors_i <- abs(cor(as.matrix(pheno_pop_i_merged[,-1:-2, with=F]), use='p'))
  cors[[pop_i]] <- cors_i
}
      
results <- list()

for (pop in populations) {
  print(pop)
  # Construct file paths and check for existence
  baseline_file <- paste0(base_dir_baseline, 'ukb.', pop, '.sparse.sscore')
  masked_file <- paste0(base_dir_masked, 'ukb.', pop, '.sparse.sscore')
  if (!file.exists(baseline_file) || !file.exists(masked_file)) next
  
  pgs_baseline <- fread(baseline_file)
  pgs_masked <- fread(masked_file)
  pgs_baseline <- pgs_baseline[pgs_baseline$IID %in% pgs_masked$IID, ]
  pgs_masked <- pgs_masked[pgs_masked$IID %in% pgs_baseline$IID, ]

  for (trait in traits) {
    print(trait)
    pheno_file <- paste0(pheno_dir, trait, '.', pop, '.txt')
    if (!file.exists(pheno_file)) next
    
    pheno <- fread(pheno_file)

    # Subset PGS data
    pgs_baseline_i <- pgs_baseline[, c('IID', names(pgs_baseline)[grepl('SUM', names(pgs_baseline)) &
                                                                    grepl(trait, names(pgs_baseline))]), with = F]
    pgs_masked_i <- pgs_masked[, c('IID', names(pgs_masked)[grepl('SUM', names(pgs_masked)) &
                                                              grepl(trait, names(pgs_masked))]), with = F]
    
    # Merge PGS data
    pgs_baseline_pheno <- merge(pheno, pgs_baseline_i, by = 'IID', sort = FALSE)
    pgs_masked_pheno <- merge(pheno, pgs_masked_i, by = 'IID', sort = FALSE)
    
    param_cols <- names(pgs_baseline_i)[-1]

    for (param in param_cols) {
      if (!(param %in% names(pgs_masked_pheno))) next
      
      r2_baseline_pheno <- cor(pgs_baseline_pheno[[trait]], pgs_baseline_pheno[[param]])^2
      r2_masked_pheno <- cor(pgs_masked_pheno[[trait]], pgs_masked_pheno[[param]])^2
      
      # Get p-value for the PGS term in the baseline model
      mod_baseline <- lm(pgs_baseline_pheno[[trait]] ~ pgs_baseline_pheno[[param]])
      slope_baseline <- coef(summary(mod_baseline))[2,1]
      p_val <- coef(summary(mod_baseline))[2,4]
      
      mod_masked <- lm(pgs_masked_pheno[[trait]] ~ pgs_masked_pheno[[param]])
      slope_masked <- coef(summary(mod_masked))[2,1]

      # Calculate correlation between scores for the estimated metric
      base_masked_mod <- lm(scale(pgs_baseline_pheno[[param]]) ~ scale(pgs_masked_pheno[[param]]))
      cor_base_masked <- coef(summary(base_masked_mod))[2,1]
      cor_base_masked_se <- coef(summary(base_masked_mod))[2,2]

      results[[length(results) + 1]] <- data.table(
          param = param,
          trait = trait,
          population = pop,
          var_baseline = var(pgs_baseline_pheno[[param]]),
          var_masked = var(pgs_masked_pheno[[param]]),
          r2_baseline_pheno = r2_baseline_pheno,
          r2_masked_pheno = r2_masked_pheno,
          cor_baseline_masked = cor_base_masked,
          cor_baseline_masked_se = cor_base_masked_se,
          p_baseline_pheno = p_val,
          slope_baseline = slope_baseline,
          slope_masked = slope_masked,
          n = nrow(pgs_baseline_pheno)
      )
    }
  }
}

results_dt <- rbindlist(results)

# Identify the PGS method for plotting
results_dt[, pgs_method := ifelse(str_detect(param, 'sbayesrc'), 'SBayesRC', 'pT+clump')]
results_dt$param<-gsub('.*_','',gsub('_SUM','',results_dt$param))

# --- 2. Calculate Final Metrics and Filter ---

# Calculate the observed and estimated relative R2 from the new metrics
results_dt[, obs_rel_r2 := r2_masked_pheno / r2_baseline_pheno]
results_dt[, est_rel_r2 := cor_baseline_masked^2]
results_dt[, obs_slope_ratio := slope_masked/slope_baseline]
results_dt[, cor_diff := 
             sqrt(results_dt$r2_masked_pheno) - sqrt(results_dt$r2_baseline_pheno)]
results_dt[, cor_diff_p := paired.r(
  xy=sqrt(results_dt$r2_baseline_pheno), 
  xz=sqrt(results_dt$r2_masked_pheno), 
  yz=results_dt$cor_baseline_masked, 
  n=results_dt$n, 
  twotailed=T)$p
]

results_dt$cor_diff_p[results_dt$obs_rel_r2 == 1] <- 1-0.001
results_dt$cor_diff_p[results_dt$cor_diff_p == 1]<-1-0.001
results_dt$cor_diff_z<-qnorm(results_dt$cor_diff_p/2)
results_dt$cor_diff_se<-abs(results_dt$cor_diff/results_dt$cor_diff_z)

# Aggregate results accounting for correlation between phenotypes
meta_res <- NULL
for(pop_i in unique(results_dt$population)){
  for(param_i in unique(results_dt$param)){
    results_dt_i<-results_dt[results_dt$population == pop_i & results_dt$param == param_i,]
    cors_i <- cors[[pop_i]][unique(results_dt_i$trait), unique(results_dt_i$trait)]
    results_dt_i$Sample<-'A'
  
    meta_res_comp_i <-
      agg(
        id = Sample,
        es = cor_diff,
        var = cor_diff_se ^ 2,
        cor = cors_i,
        method = "BHHR",
        mod = NULL,
        data = results_dt_i
    )
    
    meta_res_comp_i_est <-
      agg(
        id = Sample,
        es = cor_baseline_masked,
        var = cor_baseline_masked_se ^ 2,
        cor = cors_i,
        method = "BHHR",
        mod = NULL,
        data = results_dt_i
    )
  
    tmp <- data.table(param = param_i,
                      pop = pop_i,
                      obs_r_diff = meta_res_comp_i$es,
                      obs_r_diff_se = sqrt(meta_res_comp_i$var),
                      est_r_diff = meta_res_comp_i_est$es,
                      est_r_diff_se = sqrt(meta_res_comp_i_est$var))
    
    meta_res <- rbind(meta_res, tmp)
  }
}

meta_res$lowCI<-meta_res$obs_r_diff - (meta_res$obs_r_diff_se*1.96)
meta_res$highCI<-meta_res$obs_r_diff + (meta_res$obs_r_diff_se*1.96)

meta_res$param[!grepl('SBayesRC', meta_res$param)] <- paste0('pt+clump: ', meta_res$param[!grepl('SBayesRC', meta_res$param)])
meta_res$param<-factor(meta_res$param, levels = unique(meta_res$param))

ggplot(meta_res[meta_res$param == 'SBayesRC',], aes(x = pop, y = obs_r_diff, colour = pop)) +
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 1), size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = lowCI,
      ymax = highCI
    ),
    width = 0.05,
    position = position_dodge(width = 1)
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = seq(1.5, length(unique(meta_res$pop))), linetype="dotted") +
  labs(x = 'Population', y = "R difference (95% CI)", colour = '') +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

############
# Look at observed relative R2 across PGS methods in EUR
############

ggplot(meta_res[meta_res$pop == 'EUR',], aes(x = param, y = obs_r_diff, colour = param)) +
  geom_point(position = position_dodge(width = 1), size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = lowCI,
      ymax = highCI
    ),
    width = 0.05,
    position = position_dodge(width = 1)
  ) +
  labs(x = '', y = "R difference (95% CI)", colour = '') +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))

###########
# Look at observed and estimated R
###########
# Estimate R based on PGS R and observed baseline R2

meta_res$est_r_masked <- meta_res$

ggplot(results_clean, aes(x = param, y = obs_rel_r2, fill = param)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  labs(x = '', y = "Observed Relative R2") +
  facet_grid(. ~ population) +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))

# Average results across phenotypes
results_dt_summary <- results_clean[, .(
    mean_obs_rel_r2 = mean(obs_rel_r2, na.rm = TRUE),
    obs_ci_lower = mean(obs_rel_r2, na.rm = TRUE) - 1.96 * (sd(obs_rel_r2, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(.N)),
    obs_ci_upper = mean(obs_rel_r2, na.rm = TRUE) + 1.96 * (sd(obs_rel_r2, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(.N))
), by = .(param, population)]

results_dt_summary$param <- factor(results_dt_summary$param, levels = unique(results_dt_summary$param))

ggplot(results_dt_summary, aes(x = param, y = mean_obs_rel_r2, fill = param)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = obs_ci_lower, ymax = obs_ci_upper), width = 0.01) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  labs(x = '', y = "Observed Relative R2") +
  facet_grid(. ~ population) +
  theme_half_open() +
  panel_border() +
  background_grid() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))


# --- Enhanced Feature: More robust filtering ---
# Filter based on both statistical significance (p < 0.05) AND a more stringent
# minimum effect size (baseline partial R² > 0.5%) to remove noisy, weakly predictive scores.
results_clean <- results_dt[p_baseline_pheno < 0.001 & r2_baseline_pheno > 0.02]

# Remove MID and AMR populations as the same size is small, and admixture complicates interpretation
#results_clean <- results_clean[results_clean$population %in% c('AFR','CSA','EAS','EUR'),]

# --- 3. Generate Visualizations ---

# Plot 1: The "Money Plot" - Estimated vs. Observed Relative R²
# This plot is the core validation of your analytical estimator.
validation_plot <- ggplot(results_clean, aes(x = est_rel_r2, y = obs_rel_r2)) +
    geom_point(aes(color = population, size = r2_baseline_pheno), alpha = 0.7) +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed", size = 1) +
    facet_wrap(~pgs_method) +
    scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
    scale_size_continuous(name = "Baseline R²", labels = scales::percent) +
    coord_cartesian(
      xlim = c(min(results_clean$est_rel_r2, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.98, NA),
      ylim = c(min(results_clean$obs_rel_r2, na.rm = TRUE) * 0.98, NA) # Zoom in but allow some points > 100%
    ) +
    labs(
        title = "Validation: Estimated vs. Observed Relative R²",
        subtitle = "Filtered for baseline PGS with p < 0.001 and partial R² > 2%",
        x = "Estimated Relative R² (cor(PGS_baseline, PGS_masked)²)",
        y = "Observed Relative Phenotypic R² ((R²_masked) / (R²_baseline))",
        color = "Population"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 14)

print(validation_plot)

# Calculate the mean and standard error for each group
results_summary <- results_clean[, .(
    mean_obs_rel_r2 = mean(obs_rel_r2, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_obs_rel_r2 = sd(obs_rel_r2, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(.N),
    mean_est_rel_r2 = mean(est_rel_r2, na.rm = TRUE),
    se_est_rel_r2 = sd(est_rel_r2, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(.N)
), by = .(pgs_method, population)]


# --- 4. Generate Visualizations ---

# Plot 1: Aggregated "Money Plot" with Error Bars
validation_plot_agg <- ggplot(results_summary, aes(x = mean_est_rel_r2, y = mean_obs_rel_r2, color = population)) +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed", size = 1) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_obs_rel_r2 - se_obs_rel_r2, ymax = mean_obs_rel_r2 + se_obs_rel_r2), width = 0.01) +
    geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = mean_est_rel_r2 - se_est_rel_r2, xmax = mean_est_rel_r2 + se_est_rel_r2), height = 0.01) +
    geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.8) +
    facet_wrap(~pgs_method) +
    scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
    labs(
        title = "Aggregated Validation: Estimated vs. Observed Relative R²",
        subtitle = "Points show mean across traits, error bars show standard error.",
        x = "Estimated Relative R² (cor(PGS_baseline, PGS_masked)²)",
        y = "Observed Relative Phenotypic R² ((Partial R²_masked) / (Partial R²_baseline))",
        color = "Population"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 14)

print(validation_plot_agg)

# Plot 2: Portability Across Ancestries
# This plot clearly shows how performance drops in different populations.
portability_plot <- ggplot(results_clean, aes(x = population, y = obs_rel_r2, fill = population)) +
    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.9) +
    facet_wrap(~pgs_method) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
    labs(
        title = "Observed PGS Portability Across Ancestries (Adjusted for Covariates)",
        subtitle = "Performance drop when masking variants with INFO < 0.95 (filtered for strong PGS)",
        x = "Population",
        y = "Observed Relative Phenotypic R²"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), legend.position = "none")

print(portability_plot)

```

***


```{r}
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)
library(scales) # For formatting plot axes

# --- 0. Bootstrap Configuration ---
# Set the number of bootstrap replicates
# 1000 is a good standard. Reduce to ~100 for a quick test run.
n_boot <- 100 
# Set a seed for reproducible results
set.seed(123) 

# --- 1. Data Processing Loop (with Bootstrapping) ---

# Define analysis parameters (as you had)
populations <- c('AFR', 'AMR', 'CSA', 'EAS', 'EUR', 'MID')
traits <- c('HT', 'HB', 'PLT', 'BMI', 'BWT', 'MCHC', 'SBP', 'NEU', 'HDL', 'TC')
base_dir_baseline <- '~/pgs_wgs/sparse/baseline/'
base_dir_masked <- '~/pgs_wgs/sparse/masked_random/'
pheno_dir <- '/mnt/project/mind_the_gap/phenotype/'

results <- list()
for (pop in populations) {
    print(paste("Processing Population:", pop))
    # Construct file paths and check for existence
    baseline_file <- paste0(base_dir_baseline, 'ukb.', pop, '.sparse.sscore')
    masked_file <- paste0(base_dir_masked, 'ukb.', pop, '.sparse.sscore')
    if (!file.exists(baseline_file) || !file.exists(masked_file)) next
    
    pgs_baseline <- fread(baseline_file)
    pgs_masked <- fread(masked_file)

    for (trait in traits) {
        print(paste("  ... Trait:", trait))
        pheno_file <- paste0(pheno_dir, trait, '.', pop, '.txt')
        if (!file.exists(pheno_file)) next
        
        pheno <- fread(pheno_file)

        # Subset PGS data
        pgs_baseline_i <- pgs_baseline[, c('IID', names(pgs_baseline)[grepl('SUM', names(pgs_baseline)) &
                                                                    grepl(trait, names(pgs_baseline))]), with = F]
        pgs_masked_i <- pgs_masked[, c('IID', names(pgs_masked)[grepl('SUM', names(pgs_masked)) &
                                                                grepl(trait, names(pgs_masked))]), with = F]
        
        # Merge PGS data
        pgs_baseline_pheno <- merge(pheno, pgs_baseline_i, by = 'IID', sort = FALSE)
        pgs_masked_pheno <- merge(pheno, pgs_masked_i, by = 'IID', sort = FALSE)
        
        param_cols <- names(pgs_baseline_i)[-1]

        for (param in param_cols) {
            if (!(param %in% names(pgs_masked_pheno))) next
            
            n <- nrow(pgs_baseline_pheno)
            
            # --- 1.5. BOOTSTRAPPING SECTION ---
            # We use replicate() to run the sampling n_boot times
            boot_stats <- replicate(n_boot, {
                # Sample row indices with replacement
                indices <- sample(1:n, n, replace = TRUE)
                
                # Get bootstrap samples using the indices
                # We sample from the *merged* data frames
                boot_base <- pgs_baseline_pheno[indices]
                boot_masked <- pgs_masked_pheno[indices]
                
                # Extract the specific columns for correlation
                pheno_vals_base <- boot_base[[trait]]
                pgs_vals_base <- boot_base[[param]]
                
                pheno_vals_masked <- boot_masked[[trait]]
                pgs_vals_masked <- boot_masked[[param]]
                
                # Calculate stats for this replicate
                # Use tryCatch in case a bootstrap sample has zero variance
                r2_base_boot <- tryCatch(cor(pheno_vals_base, pgs_vals_base)^2, error = function(e) NA)
                r2_masked_boot <- tryCatch(cor(pheno_vals_masked, pgs_vals_masked)^2, error = function(e) NA)
                
                # Calculate relative R2
                rel_r2_boot <- if (is.na(r2_base_boot) || is.na(r2_masked_boot) || r2_base_boot == 0) {
                    NA
                } else {
                    r2_masked_boot / r2_base_boot
                }
                
                # Return a named vector
                c(r2_base = r2_base_boot, r2_masked = r2_masked_boot, rel_r2 = rel_r2_boot)
            })
            
            # Transpose boot_stats (replicate puts stats in rows, iterations in cols)
            boot_stats_t <- t(boot_stats)
            
            # Calculate 95% CIs (using the percentile method)
            # Using na.rm = TRUE is crucial
            r2_base_ci <- quantile(boot_stats_t[, "r2_base"], c(0.025, 0.975), na.rm = TRUE)
            r2_masked_ci <- quantile(boot_stats_t[, "r2_masked"], c(0.025, 0.975), na.rm = TRUE)
            rel_r2_ci <- quantile(boot_stats_t[, "rel_r2"], c(0.025, 0.975), na.rm = TRUE)
            # --- END BOOTSTRAPPING ---
            

            # --- 1.6. Calculate Point Estimates and Store ---
            # Your original calculations, which serve as the point estimates
            r2_baseline_pheno <- cor(pgs_baseline_pheno[[trait]], pgs_baseline_pheno[[param]])^2
            r2_masked_pheno <- cor(pgs_masked_pheno[[trait]], pgs_masked_pheno[[param]])^2
            p_val <- coef(summary(lm(pgs_baseline_pheno[[trait]] ~ pgs_baseline_pheno[[param]])))[2,4]
            cor_base_masked <- cor(pgs_baseline_pheno[[param]], pgs_masked_pheno[[param]])
            obs_rel_r2_point <- r2_masked_pheno / r2_baseline_pheno

            results[[length(results) + 1]] <- data.table(
                param = param,
                trait = trait,
                population = pop,
                
                # Point estimates
                r2_baseline_pheno = r2_baseline_pheno,
                r2_masked_pheno = r2_masked_pheno,
                obs_rel_r2 = obs_rel_r2_point, # Store the point estimate for relative R2
                cor_baseline_masked = cor_base_masked,
                p_baseline_pheno = p_val,
                n = n,
                
                # R2 Baseline CIs
                r2_baseline_low_ci = r2_base_ci[1],
                r2_baseline_high_ci = r2_base_ci[2],
                
                # R2 Masked CIs
                r2_masked_low_ci = r2_masked_ci[1],
                r2_masked_high_ci = r2_masked_ci[2],
                
                # Relative R2 CIs
                rel_r2_low_ci = rel_r2_ci[1],
                rel_r2_high_ci = rel_r2_ci[2]
            )
        }
    }
}

results_dt <- rbindlist(results)
results_dt <- results_dt[p_baseline_pheno < 0.001 & r2_baseline_pheno > 0.02,]
results_dt[, pgs_method := ifelse(str_detect(param, 'sbayesrc'), 'SBayesRC', 'pT+clump')]

# Filter for one PGS method for a clearer plot, e.g., SBayesRC
plot_data_agg <- results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC']

if (nrow(plot_data_agg) > 0) {
    
    print("Generating aggregated relative R² plot...")

    plot_agg_r2 <- ggplot(plot_data_agg, aes(x = population, y = obs_rel_r2, 
                                            color = population)) +
        
        # 1. Add a horizontal line at 1.0 (100%) for "no change"
        geom_hline(yintercept = 1.0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
        
        # 2. Show individual traits as semi-transparent points
        # 'geom_jitter' spreads them out so they don't overlap
        geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.5, size = 2) +
        
        # 3. Add the summary statistic (mean and 95% CI *of the mean*)
        # This automatically aggregates across all traits for each population
        stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_cl_normal", # Uses normal approx. for CI of the mean
                     geom = "pointrange",        # Show as a point + error bar
                     size = 1.0,                 # Make the summary point larger
                     color = "black") +          # Make the summary point black
        
        labs(
            title = "Aggregated Relative R² (Masked vs. Baseline)",
            subtitle = "Points are individual traits. Black bars are the mean (± 95% CI) across all traits.\n(SBayesRC method)",
            x = "Population",
            y = "Relative R² (Masked / Baseline)"
        ) +
        theme_bw() +
        theme(legend.position = "none") + # No legend needed
        
        # Format the y-axis as percentages
        scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1))

    print(plot_agg_r2)
    # ggsave("plot_aggregated_relative_r2.png", plot_agg_r2, width = 8, height = 6)

} else {
    print("No data found for SBayesRC to generate aggregated plot.")
}

# --- 2. Calculate Final Metrics and Filter ---

# Calculate the estimated relative R2 (this is still useful to compare)
results_dt[, est_rel_r2 := cor_baseline_masked^2]

# NOTE: The 'obs_rel_r2' column is now created *inside* the loop, 
# so we don't need this line anymore:
# results_dt[, obs_rel_r2 := r2_masked_pheno / r2_baseline_pheno]

# Clean up any potential Inf values from division by zero
results_dt <- results_dt[is.finite(obs_rel_r2)]

print("Data processing and bootstrapping complete.")
print(head(results_dt))


# --- 3. Plotting with Confidence Intervals ---

#> **Note:** These plots will be much more informative now. I'll filter for one
#> `pgs_method` as an example. You can remove or change the filter as needed.

# Filter for one PGS method for a clearer plot
plot_data_sbayesrc <- results_dt[pgs_method == 'SBayesRC']


### Plot 1: Relative R² (Masked vs. Baseline) with CIs

# This plot directly addresses your request for the relative R2 CIs
# It "aggregates" by showing all traits, faceted by population.

plot_rel_r2 <- ggplot(plot_data_sbayesrc[plot_data_sbayesrc$r2_baseline_pheno > 0.02,], aes(x = trait, y = obs_rel_r2, 
                                             ymin = rel_r2_low_ci, 
                                             ymax = rel_r2_high_ci, 
                                             color = trait)) +
    # Use geom_pointrange to show the point estimate and CI range
    geom_pointrange(size = 0.6, linewidth = 0.8) +
    
    # Add a dashed line at 1.0 (100%) for "no change"
    geom_hline(yintercept = 1.0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
    
    # Facet by population to see population-specific trends
    facet_wrap(~ population, ncol = 3) +
    
    labs(
        title = "Relative R² (Masked vs. Baseline) by Population and Trait",
        subtitle = paste("Showing 95% Bootstrap CIs (", n_boot, " replicates, SBayesRC)"),
        x = "Phenotype (Trait)",
        y = "Relative R² (Masked / Baseline)"
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "none" # Hide legend; colors are just for distinction
    ) +
    # Format the y-axis as percentages
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1))

print(plot_rel_r2)
# ggsave("plot_relative_r2_ci.png", plot_rel_r2, width = 10, height = 7)


### Plot 2: Absolute R² (Baseline vs. Masked) with CIs

# To plot this, we need to "melt" the data from wide to long format
# 1. Melt baseline data
base_melt <- plot_data_sbayesrc[, 
                         .(population, trait, pgs_method, 
                           model_type = 'Baseline', 
                           r2 = r2_baseline_pheno, 
                           r2_low = r2_baseline_low_ci, 
                           r2_high = r2_baseline_high_ci)]
                           
# 2. Melt masked data
masked_melt <- plot_data_sbayesrc[,
                          .(population, trait, pgs_method,
                            model_type = 'Masked',
                            r2 = r2_masked_pheno,
                            r2_low = r2_masked_low_ci,
                            r2_high = r2_masked_high_ci)]
                            
# 3. Combine
plot_data_abs <- rbind(base_melt, masked_melt)

# 4. Plot
plot_abs_r2 <- ggplot(plot_data_abs, aes(x = trait, y = r2, 
                                        ymin = r2_low, ymax = r2_high, 
                                        color = model_type)) +
    # Use position_dodge to show baseline and masked side-by-side
    geom_pointrange(position = position_dodge(width = 0.6), size = 0.5, linewidth = 0.7) +
    
    facet_wrap(~ population, scales = "free_y", ncol = 3) + # Use "free_y" as R2 varies by pop
    labs(
        title = "Absolute PGS R² (Baseline vs. Masked)",
        subtitle = paste("Showing 95% Bootstrap CIs (", n_boot, " replicates, SBayesRC)"),
        x = "Phenotype (Trait)",
        y = "Phenotype R²",
        color = "PGS Type"
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "bottom"
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 0.1)) +
    scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") # Use a color-blind friendly palette

print(plot_abs_r2)
# ggsave("plot_absolute_r2_ci.png", plot_abs_r2, width = 10, height = 7)

plot_obs_vs_est_ci <- ggplot(results_dt[results_dt$r2_baseline_pheno > 0.05,], aes(x = est_rel_r2, y = obs_rel_r2, color = population)) +
        
        # Add vertical error bars for the Observed Relative R² CIs
        geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = rel_r2_low_ci, ymax = rel_r2_high_ci), 
                      width = 0, alpha = 0.3) +
        
        # Add the point estimate
        geom_point(alpha = 0.8, size = 2) +
        
        # Add the y=x reference line
        # Any point on this line is "perfectly estimated"
        geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black") +
        
        # Facet by the PGS method for a direct comparison
        facet_wrap(~ pgs_method) +
        
        # Force a 1:1 aspect ratio so the diagonal line is at a 45-degree angle
        coord_fixed(ratio = 1) + 
        
        labs(
            title = "Observed vs. Estimated Relative R²",
            subtitle = "Points on the dashed line (y=x) show perfect estimation.\nError bars show 95% CI for Observed Relative R².",
            x = "Estimated Relative R² (cor(PGS_base, PGS_masked)²) ",
            y = "Observed Relative R² (R²_masked / R²_baseline)",
            color = "Population"
        ) +
        theme_bw() +
        theme(
            legend.position = "right",
            plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
            strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10)
        ) +
        
        # Use a clear color palette
        scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
        
        # Format axes as percentages
        scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1)) +
        scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 1))


```
